title: HB-2/26/26 - Investment Grade Coins and Bullion
description:
About this auction-   It is 100% the bidders responsibility to be familiar with the terms used and the value is subjective and 100% the bidders due diligence responsibility without fail. The auction LOT Title and image are the definitive measure of each item. The auction title is a broad description of the items up for sale, and not the totality of the sale being offered.  We offer a reasonably fast shipping services and do NOT allow bidders to pick up items due to the nature of what we sell. Please note- the terminology " MAY HAVE WON" means that the sale isnt over on all the venues we are selling the goods on - The auctioneer record is the record of transaction.  You may still be outbid by other bidders.

1.) All items are sold as is , where is, with no refunds, discounts to bid, or refunds. 

2.) Bidder agrees that any bid placed will be honored as a contract and the credit card that you registered with will be charged by Antique Finder Services at the end of the sale.  If this is not available, we will send a  invoice that must be completed within 24 hours of receipt.  It is the responsibility of the bidder that the card  registered be able to complete the sale, or call us with an alternate means of payment within 24 hours of end of sale.

3.) Bidder agrees to pay a Buyer's Premium of 20% via Credit Card - 18% Via Paper Check or Bank Wire or ACH. All invoices more than $5000 will be required to be paid by check, bank wire or ACH -

4.) If for any reason you do not pay within 48 hours of end of auction, we will resell the items and the collected monies will go towards paying your invoice. Any shortages, you will still be liable for.

5.) Shipping will take place within 15 to 30 business days ( dependant upon where the physical sale takes place - Since we have more than one location, multiple shipments may arrive for individual invoices) from cleared payment into our bank account. Tracking will be emailed to the bidders email. We are not liable for shipping delays due to weather or press of business for the USPS or pick up delays. Bidder agrees to a NON CHARGEBACK transaction. If for ANY reason a client charges back - the original invoice, plus 100% fee will be charged to the clients account.  

6.)  All items will be described in detail and have at least 1 high resolution image for you to base your valuation on. We do not control the price of the item, and cannot accept returns, unless not as described. Example... you purchased an item and the incorrect item was sent to you. Such as 14k gold ring is not 14k gold. 

7.) Any and all legal recourse for either party will take place in Snohomish County Washington and we reserve the right to charge for the legal fees. Any liability by seller never is more than the original value of any invoice  Bidder agrees to a NON CHARGEBACK transaction. Shipping delays are common in the metals industry and are NOT a reason for a chargeback. We guarantee full and complete invoice shipping. We cannot control shipping times on some bullion items, or out of country delays. 


AUCTION TERMINOLOGY:

BU = Brilliant uncirculated  

ASE = American silver eagle 

NON BULLION = Means nothing further can be rendered from this product 

Gold Leaf = A composite of metals with very little gold content, hammered flat to a milimeter thickness to be used for gold leafing of articles -  These Gold Leaf Flakes are made from a Composition of scrap metal flakes from A Gold leafing manufacturer.  There is no melt value to these flakes and they are Not for precious metal investment. These Gold Flakes are NOT edible gold leaf. 

Gold Leaf Scrap = Gold leaf cuttings from the above mentioned product

Alluvial Gold= Gold from natural earth mining via streams or rocks 

Natural = From the earth with accepted treatments to bring out value and vibrance 

Electronically Tested = Means that the item will need to be further evaluated to confirm whether it is natural or man made. Electronically tested items have all the same chemical composition and elements as a natural item 

Random date = means random from the dates we have available at the time of the sale, with no guarantee as to specific dates -

BU and Proof Coins are coins from US mint and proof sets - and never in circulation -

title: 2/26/2026 - 3081 Waccamaw Blvd. Myrtle Beach, SC 29579
description:
Online-Only Consignment Auction
John T. Henry Auction Co. LLC
3081 Waccamaw Blvd., Myrtle Beach, SC 29579
Thursday, February 26th at 2PM
________________________________________
Important Shipping Update!!!

All shipping arrangements must now be made through Shipping Saint. After the auction closes, all customers will receive a text message to confirm either their pickup time or their shipping details.

Shipping Saint is now fully integrated with our trusted shipping provider, Carolina Services & Crating. This integration allows the shipping company to receive detailed order information for each customer. They will correspond with you directly via text regarding shipping bill payment, provide tracking information, and keep you updated on the shipping process from start to finish.
________________________________________
Auction Location & Schedule:

3081 Waccamaw Blvd., Myrtle Beach, SC 29579
Preview is available on Thursday, February 26th from 9-3PM
The auction will begin closing on Thursday, February 26th at 2PM
Item pickup will take place Friday, February 27th from 8:30AM - 3PM

Buyers will receive a text after the auction with a link to confirm pickup or request shipping. If picking up in person, use the scheduler link provided in the text to reserve a pickup time.
Payment Information:
Credit cards will be charged immediately following the auction for a nonrefundable deposit of up to $2000. The remaining balance must be paid in full on pickup day. Payment is required before any items can be removed.
__________________________________________________
Shipping:
Shipping is available for all items and is handled through Carolina Relocation Services (CRS). If you need shipping, reply to the post-auction text or to your paid-in-full invoice. Items for shipping will be picked up by CRS at our facility and transported to their warehouse at 6720 Elbow Road, Conway, SC 29527. CRS will contact you directly to arrange packing, quotes, and delivery.
jthpackers@oulook.com
843-349-1070
_________________________________________________
Important Terms and Conditions
All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no guarantees, refunds, or returns.
A 13% buyer's premium applies to all purchases.
An 8% sales tax will be collected unless a valid retail sales license is on file.
Descriptions are believed to be accurate but are not guaranteed. We make every effort not to mislead any buyer, and we strive to identify any imperfections or counterfeit items. However, we do not catch everything, and any oversight is never intentional. We advise consignors to perform due diligence before submitting items for sale.
Bidders are strongly encouraged to attend preview days for inspection and to call or email with any questions or concerns prior to bidding. By placing a bid, bidders accept the item's condition and agree to all terms. Please bid accordingly.
Item Removal
Buyers are responsible for removing their purchases during the scheduled pickup window.
Items not picked up by the deadline will be considered abandoned. Abandoned items may be resold, and no refunds will be issued. The auction company is not responsible for damage or theft after the auction ends.
Transportation
Buyers must make their own arrangements for transporting items. We do not provide moving services.
Shipping is available through CRS only.
Registration
By registering, bidders agree to all terms and conditions.
Failure to comply may result in the loss of bidding privileges.
__________________________________________________
Final Notes:
New items are accepted for future auctions immediately after this one closes. Space is limited, so all purchased items must be removed on time. We appreciate your cooperation in helping us keep the auction process smooth and organized.

description:

We have approx 700 items from a major online retailer and the usual mix of unique items from our consignors! 

All lots start at $5 with no reserve!

ONLINE ONLY - ONLINE ONLY - ONLINE ONLY

Transfer to the Pflugerville warehouse is available on almost all items for an extra 10% added to your buyer's premium. Pallet lots are not eligible for transfer to Pflugerville. See lot details.

ALL INVOICES MUST BE PAID BY 3PM ON SATURDAY FEBRUARY 28TH All invoices not paid by 3 PM on Saturday February 28th will be billed to your card on file.

You will be able to pay your invoice in person (cash or credit card) or an online payment link will be available upon request - simply reply to the invoice email you will receive after the auction and let us know if you will be picking up in Alice or in Pflugerville.

All invoice requiring transfer to Pflugerville must be paid by 3 PM February 28th or will not ship. 

Contact Steve via email at steve@atxauctions.com with any questions.

This auction is online only and will take place on Thursday February 26th. Lots will start to close at 1:15 PM on the 26th and will close at a rate of approx 4 per minute until all lots are sold.

Pickup in Alice is located at our warehouse:

36 S Johnson, 
Alice, TX  78332

on Friday February 27th from 10 AM to 6PM & Saturday February 28th from 10 AM to 4 PM.

Delivery to the Pflugerville location is offered for this auction on most lots. Pallet lots are not eligible for shipping to Pflugerville - see lot details. Pick up in Pflugerville is scheduled for the following Friday March 6th from 10 am to 6 pm and Saturday the 7th from 10AM to 5 PM - at our location in Pflugerville - 309 North Railroad in the Mokan Storage facility. Alternate pickup times for Pflugerville can be arranged but must be coordinated in advance.

Our address in Pflugerville is 
309 N. Railroad. We are located in the Mokan Storage facility.

If you will be requiring shipping to Pflugerville, please call us to arrange payment and shipping. Your invoice must be paid before we can ship to Pflugerville.

We WILL NOT be shipping any items from this auction to any other location (beside Pflugerville) or via FedEx / UPS / Freight / etc. !!!  If you cannot pick up on the Alice or Pflugerville dates please refrain from bidding in this auction.

If you are not already on our text list you can join by texting "ATXALICE" to 289289. There is no cost to you to join and we never share your information with anyone else. Message and data rates may apply. We send text messages only twice per auction (once when it goes up and another 2 hours before it starts to close).

title: Small Lot for Sale in Taylorsville NC
description:
Small Lot for Sale in Taylorsville NC
Offered at auction with reserve, this 1.9± acre property is located in the desirable Sugar Loaf / Vashti community of Alexander County, NC. The property is zoned R-2R and has no deed restrictions, providing flexibility for a variety of residential uses. Several mobile homes and outbuildings are currently on the property and are conveyed with the sale. Any personal property, contents, or miscellaneous items located within the mobile homes and outbuildings will also convey.
A septic tank is in place, and a water tap is available, providing valuable utility access for future improvements. This property offers a great opportunity for buyers seeking space, flexibility, and utility access in a peaceful rural setting while remaining convenient to Taylorsville and the surrounding areas. The property is being sold AS IS, WHERE IS, with buyers encouraged to complete all desired inspections prior to bidding.
Online Auction Ends Thursday, February 26th, at 4 PM
21 Hickory Dr., Taylorsville, NC 28681
CONTACT: Broker & Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at (540) 239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Broker & Auctioneer Sharon Roseman with United Country Big 6 Properties at (828) 320-4726 or email SharonCRoseman@gmail.com.
DIRECTIONS: From Interstate 77 in Statesville, take Exit 49A and follow US Highway 64/NC Highway 90 West toward Taylorsville. Continue on Hwy 64/90 and turn right onto NC Highway 16 North. Travel north on Hwy 16, then turn right onto Vashti Road.  At Stop Sign Turn left to Follow Vashti Road. Hickory Drive will be on your left and is a shared drive. Continue straight to 21 Hickory Drive, which will be on the road. Look for auction signage.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of $500 is offered to a cooperating NC State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $2,500 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before April 13th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on February 26th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBig6.com

title: Living Estate of Patricia Campbell
description: We are pleased to present the Living Estate of Patricia Campbell Auction, a thoughtfully curated selection of quality furnishings, decorative pieces, and unique finds from a well-kept home. While modest in size, this sale is full of charm, character, and timeless appeal.Highlights include a beautiful roll-top desk with ample storage, Mission-style oak furniture, and other classic wooden pieces perfect for home or office. You’ll also find a selection of vintage and decorative lighting, including brass lamps and unique repurposed items such as a Fyr-Fyter fire extinguisher lamp.This auction features a tasteful mix of home décor, including blue and white pieces, decorative accessories, and practical furnishings that blend function with style. Each lot reflects a lifetime of careful ownership and offers something for collectors, decorators, and everyday buyers alike.Whether you are looking to furnish a space, add a special piece to your collection, or simply enjoy bidding, this is a quality estate auction you won’t want to miss.Bid now and bring home something special.

title: RODABAUGH ESTATE ONLINE AUCTION
description:
Thomas & Esther Rodabaugh Estate Online Auction

Adam Breault Auctions is proud to announce the online liquidation auction of the Thomas and Esther Rodabaugh estate. This extensive collection features a wide variety of high-quality items, ranging from mid-century modern home decor and fine furniture to a complete woodshop and unique specialty collectibles.

AUCTION SCHEDULE:
- Bidding Ends: Thursday, February 26, 2026 @ 7 PM
- Item Pickup: Saturday, February 28, 2026, from 10 AM - 2 PM
- Pickup Location: 3154 Traxler Drive, Beaverton, MI 48612

AUCTION HIGHLIGHTS:
- FURNITURE & HOME DECOR: Stackable washer/dryer unit, sofas, dressers, nightstands, curio cabinets, dining tables, electric fireplace, mirrors, and office furniture. Features vintage and MCM light fixtures, artwork, wall hangings, and cuckoo clocks.
- WOODSHOP & TOOLS: Jointer planer, scroll saw, chop saw, router combo table, drill press, bench grinder, tool chests, shop vacs, and a large selection of power tools (circular saws, jigsaws, reciprocating saws, and more).
- COLLECTIBLES & SPECIALTY: Military memorabilia, vintage GM emblems, die-cast automobiles, figurines, and a unique collection of clown and magician costumes, props, and accessories.
- ELECTRONICS & HOBBIES: Digital piano, flatscreen TV, laptop, cameras, acoustic guitar, and jewelry-making supplies. Full sewing suite including machines, thread, yarn, and boxes.
- KITCHEN & HOUSEWARES: KitchenAid mixer, electric roaster, buffet server, vintage glassware (porcelain, pottery, crystal), and a variety of cookware and bakeware.
-JEWLERY & SUPPLIES: A mixture of sterling silver and diamond necklaces and bracelets as well as costume jewelry and jewelery making supplies.

VIEW CATALOG & BID ONLINE: For complete details, terms, and hundreds of photos, please visit: ADAMBREAULT.COM

title: THU WKLY #4 COINS DEPARTMENT 56 FURNITURE PYREX MORE
description:
FINAL: PREVIEW IS TUE,WED,THU 9-5 CLOSED 12-1 PU IS FRI SAT & SUN 10-5 CLOSED 12-1

WE SHIP IN HOUSE.  THIS MEANS BETTER RATES, FASTER TURN OVER FOR SHIPPING.  YOU WILL RECEIVE A TEXT MESSAGE OR EMAIL POST AUCTION.  IF YOU NEED SHIPPING FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS.

PLEASE PICKUP ITEMS BY END OF DAY SUNDAY 5PM FOLLOWING THE AUCTION.  

IF YOU CAN NOT THERE ARE OPTIONS FOR SMALLER ITEMS. FURNITURE WILL NOT BE ALLOWED FOR LATE PICKUP AS WE HAVE LIMITED SPACE.

1. EMAIL AT TYLERGRACEAUCTIONS@GMAIL.COM AND ASK FOR A LATE PICKUP. 

2. REQUEST SHIPPING THROUGH LINKS PROVIDED

3. CONTACT A MOVER OF YOURS OR A RECOMMENDED MOVER AT THE BOTTOM OF YOUR INVOICE PAGE.

IF YOU DO NONE OF THESE AND JUST ABANDON YOUR ITEMS THEY WILL BE RELISTED AND YOUR ITEMS AND MONIES WILL BE FORFEITED.

SHIPPING 

THERE WILL BE A LINK PROVIDED IN THE EMAIL AND OR TEXT MESSAGES FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS PROVIDED.

AFTER 48 HOURS IF NO RESPONSE WE WILL JUST SHIP USING THE CREDIT CARD ON FILE, BUT THIS SLOWS THE PROCESS DOWN, YOU DONT GET TEXT MESSAGES WITH TRACKING UPDATES BECAUSE YOU DIDNT CLICK THE LINK SENT TO YOU.   BUT WE CAN NOT HOLD ONTO ITEMS WAITING ON THAT PROCESS.   

IF NO CONTACT HAS BEEN MADE AND THE PAYMENT DOESNT GO THROUGH FOR SHIPPING, NORMAL ABANDONMENT TIMES AND FORFEITURE POLICIES WILL APPLY.

PAYMENT

I REQUIRE A GOOD CREDIT CARD ON FILE TO BID THAT CAN BE CHARGED THE NIGHT OF AUCTION.   IN THE EVENT A CREDIT CARD DOESN'T GO THROUGH THE NIGHT OF AUCTION THERE WILL BE A LINK SENT OVER TO PAY VIA ZELLE.  IF THAT INVOICE IS NOT PAID THE NIGHT OF.  THERE WILL BE A $10 INCONVENIENCE FEE ADDED.  IF THE BALANCE IS NOT PAID FOR BIDDING PRIVILEGES WILL BE SUSPENDED IN HIBID.   

IF YOU ARE BIDDING ON GOLD OR POTENTIALLY OTHER EXPENSIVE ITEMS .  I WILL REQUIRE A ZELLE OR CASH PAYMENT ALWAYS IF YOU ARE A NEW BIDDER OR OUT OF STATE.   AND POSSIBLY IF YOU ARE LOCAL.   IT WILL BE AT THE AUCTIONEERS DISCRETION IF I CHOOSE NOT TO.   ESTABLISHED BIDDERS WILL NOT HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT THIS. 

WE DO NOT TAKE OUT OF STATE TAX PERMITS. AND WE CHECK VALIDITY OF IN STATE TAX PERMITS PRIOR TO REMOVING SALES TAX.

RECEIPT OF ITEMS AND SIGNING OF INVOICE.

MOST AUCTIONS HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  IN HOME ESTATES DO NOT HAVE THIS ABILITY AND THEREFORE YOU ARE BIDDING ON AN ITEM BASED ON THE PICTURES AND DESCRIPTION GIVEN KNOWING THAT AN ITEM IF NOT LISTED AS NEW MAY HAVE WEAR, TEAR, OR OTHER POSSIBLE DEFECTS.    THIS MEANS UNLESS I MAKE A MISTAKE SAY I LIST A TV AS A MICROWAVE, A TWIN BED AS A FULL OR SIMILAR OBVIOUS MISTAKE THEN ALL ITEMS ARE AS IS WHERE IS WITH NO WARRANTY EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.   

IF IT IS AT THE AUCTION HOUSE.   A PREVIEW IS ALWAYS OFFERED, THERE IS NO EXCUSE FOR NOT KNOWING THE CONDITION OF AN ITEM.  IF THERE IS A DISCREPANCY THEN IT NEEDS TO BE DISCUSSED PRIOR TO BEING TAKEN OFF PREMISES.   ONCE THE INVOICE IS SIGNED YOU ARE AGREEING THAT THE ITEM IS ACCEPTABLE IN ITS DESCRIPTION AND FORM AND NO FURTHER DISCUSSION WILL OCCUR ON THE MATTER.   ULTIMATELY I AM THE FINAL SAY ON THE MATTER AND RESERVE THAT RIGHT.

title: February Gallery Estate Auction
description:
We have another amazing auction for you! The gallery is packed again with an incredible collection from a single estate. This is the second in a series of auctions to help this hill country family liquidate the inventory from two previous antique stores and the items from their personal collection. These auctions feature selections from a local antique store and the family's New Zealand antique shop.
There is a special section of lots that were donated by a rural volunteer fire fighter. Lots 161 to 171 are a donation for the Ft. McAvett Volunteer Fire Department. 100% of hammer price for lots 161 to 171 will be donated to FMVFD. Bid high....it's for a good cause!

Pick up Saturday February 28th at our gallery. 9184 FM 1283 Lakehills, Tx 78063

This spacious 4-bedroom, 3.5-bath home. with approx 3000 ht sq ft, offers character, flexibility, and standout features throughout. The home includes a finished basement with a living area, full bathroom, and bedroom, ideal for guests, extended family, or a private retreat. The basement also has infrastructure for a wood-burning stove, offering the potential for cozy supplemental heat. Outside, the property features an in-ground pool that is currently non-functional and will need a new liner and minor upgrades—an excellent opportunity to create a personalized backyard oasis. Located in an established Waldron neighborhood, the home is close to schools, shopping, dining, and medical facilities, with easy access to the Ouachita National Forest and outdoor recreation. With its generous layout and strong potential, 1081 Warren Street is ready for your vision. 
Schedule your private showing today.

title: HB-2/28/26- Select Coins and Bullion
description:
About this auction-   It is 100% the bidders responsibility to be familiar with the terms used and the value is subjective and 100% the bidders due diligence responsibility without fail. The auction LOT Title and image are the definitive measure of each item. The auction title is a broad description of the items up for sale, and not the totality of the sale being offered.  We offer a reasonably fast shipping services and do NOT allow bidders to pick up items due to the nature of what we sell. Please note- the terminology " MAY HAVE WON" means that the sale isnt over on all the venues we are selling the goods on - The auctioneer record is the record of transaction.  You may still be outbid by other bidders.

1.) All items are sold as is , where is, with no refunds, discounts to bid, or refunds. 

2.) Bidder agrees that any bid placed will be honored as a contract and the credit card that you registered with will be charged by Antique Finder Services at the end of the sale.  If this is not available, we will send a  invoice that must be completed within 24 hours of receipt.  It is the responsibility of the bidder that the card  registered be able to complete the sale, or call us with an alternate means of payment within 24 hours of end of sale.

3.) Bidder agrees to pay a Buyer's Premium of 20% via Credit Card - 18% Via Paper Check or Bank Wire or ACH. All invoices more than $5000 will be required to be paid by check, bank wire or ACH -

4.) If for any reason you do not pay within 48 hours of end of auction, we will resell the items and the collected monies will go towards paying your invoice. Any shortages, you will still be liable for.

5.) Shipping will take place within 15 to 30 business days ( dependant upon where the physical sale takes place - Since we have more than one location, multiple shipments may arrive for individual invoices) from cleared payment into our bank account. Tracking will be emailed to the bidders email. We are not liable for shipping delays due to weather or press of business for the USPS or pick up delays. Bidder agrees to a NON CHARGEBACK transaction. If for ANY reason a client charges back - the original invoice, plus 100% fee will be charged to the clients account.  

6.)  All items will be described in detail and have at least 1 high resolution image for you to base your valuation on. We do not control the price of the item, and cannot accept returns, unless not as described. Example... you purchased an item and the incorrect item was sent to you. Such as 14k gold ring is not 14k gold. 

7.) Any and all legal recourse for either party will take place in Snohomish County Washington and we reserve the right to charge for the legal fees. Any liability by seller never is more than the original value of any invoice  Bidder agrees to a NON CHARGEBACK transaction. Shipping delays are common in the metals industry and are NOT a reason for a chargeback. We guarantee full and complete invoice shipping. We cannot control shipping times on some bullion items, or out of country delays. 


AUCTION TERMINOLOGY:

BU = Brilliant uncirculated  

ASE = American silver eagle 

NON BULLION = Means nothing further can be rendered from this product 

Gold Leaf = A composite of metals with very little gold content, hammered flat to a milimeter thickness to be used for gold leafing of articles -  These Gold Leaf Flakes are made from a Composition of scrap metal flakes from A Gold leafing manufacturer.  There is no melt value to these flakes and they are Not for precious metal investment. These Gold Flakes are NOT edible gold leaf. 

Gold Leaf Scrap = Gold leaf cuttings from the above mentioned product

Alluvial Gold= Gold from natural earth mining via streams or rocks 

Natural = From the earth with accepted treatments to bring out value and vibrance 

Electronically Tested = Means that the item will need to be further evaluated to confirm whether it is natural or man made. Electronically tested items have all the same chemical composition and elements as a natural item 

Random date = means random from the dates we have available at the time of the sale, with no guarantee as to specific dates -

BU and Proof Coins are coins from US mint and proof sets - and never in circulation -

If you are coming in person to pick up, you will need to bring your own totes, packaging and labor. ** IF ANY BUYER DOES NOT PICK UP THEIR ITEMS PURCHASED BY ANNOUNCED DATE & TIME, BUYER LOSES OWNERSHIP AND SELLERS TAKE BACK OWNERSHIP, WITH NO REFUND, UNLESS SPECIAL ARRANGEMENTS HAVE BEEN MADE BETWEEN THE BUYER, AUCTION COMPANY AND SELLER.

**SHIPPING: We will transport items for all our out of state bidders to All In Shipping (Jami 618.235.6080) on the Wednesday following the end of auction. There they will package and ship your items. The exact shipping charges will be processed through your HiBid account. You will receive tracking information shortly thereafter.

***LOCAL DELIVERY NOW AVAILABLE!  Need delivery?  Call Adam at 618-234-8751 for a quote!  Local (25 miles or less) delivery starting at $50.  Over 26 miles, call Adam for a quote.  Please note that charges can vary based on location, number of items, size, and weight. ALL delivery quotes and appointments MUST be scheduled with Adam at 618-234-8751.

title: Living Estate In Knightdale(Names Withheld Upon Request)
description:
This is the Fourth of several auctions to come in Knightdale, NC. This is a living estate; the name has been withheld upon request. Only the winning bidders will be given the address.

In this auction will be an assortment of  Pictures and prints NIP items for Valentine's Day gifts such as: gloves, plaques,  home decor, children's toys, candle holders, shelving items, figurines,outside decor , vintage and antique glasswareand much more. 

This auction will run from February 14, 2026 to February 28, 2026. The first lot will end on February 28, 2026 at 5pm.  We will only have 1 day for pickup.  Pickup will be on Friday March 6, 2026. Pickup will  from 9 am until 1 pm. We will try to call the winning bidders when we have all their items pulled. Please bring your own boxes, paper, and help to get your auction winnings.

NOTICE: THIS IS A LIVING ESTATE. PEOPLE ARE STILL LIVING THERE. NO ONE WILL BE ALLOWED INTO THE HOUSE. WE WILL HAVE EVERYTHING IN A BOX AND WILL HAND IT TO YOU.

 PLEASE DO NOT BID IF YOU ARE UNABLE TO PICK UP ON THESE DAYS.

You can click the following link to join the auction.....somewhereintime.hibid.com

If you have any questions please email them to somewhereintimeauctionhouse@gmail.com or you can call 252-458-4579 between 9 am and 4 pm.

Happing Bidding

Somewhere In Time Auction House

title: 229: Sports Cards, Vintage & modern, non-sports, collectible
description:
Please Note: We use a scanner for a lot of the cards in the auction. The lines on the card are from the plastic sleeves. You will find a huge selection of the Hottest RC's and Stars from today and yesterday.  Our inventory is very large and covers a wide variety from Vintage to Modern to Non-Sport in a variety of price ranges.  From High-End to $1 and Bulk deals, we have something for everyone.  You will usually find something you have never seen before, as we try to keep the inventory fresh and unique.
We add new items daily, so check back for additional items up to the start of the auction.
For single card orders under $10, we offer $2.99 PWE shipping.  For orders over $10, minimum shipping cost per order $6.50.   Orders Over $100.00 will require insurance at a small additional charge based on amount over $100.   Heavy orders will be charged by weight, and we extend our shipping discounts to our customers.  We strive to have the most affordable shipping rates for our customers.
All invoices will be sent 24-48 hours after the close of the auction and will have shipping charges included. Invoices will be charged to the credit cards on file upon time of invoicing.  All orders will be shipped out within 3-5 Business Days.  PLEASE ENSURE YOUR SHIPPING ADDRESS AND EMAIL ADDRESS ON FILE ARE UP TO DATE.  If your account has multiple addresses on file, we will make 2 attempts (1 by email, 1 by phone) to verify your accurate shipping address.  It is the buyer's responsibility to respond to address verifications within 5 business days of contact.  We will hold orders for 7 days.

title: Yarber Tools & More Round 2 Estate Auction of Oak Ridge
description:
Charles and Jon are proud to bring you another great auction for JD's Realty and Auction!
Explore a treasure trove of estate finds in our latest online-only auction from JD’s!
Highlights include:
-Woodworking Tools
-Vintage Tools
-Craft Supplies
-Fishing Lures & Tackle
-Outdoor Decorations
-Vintage Road Signs
-And several other items.

NOTICE: This Garage/Workshop is located at the back of the property.  The yard is too soft for vehicles.  All items will have to be brought curbside by hand.  Be sure to bring plenty of help to move and load any items you win.  We will NOT allow any vehicles in the yard.

For questions concerning items in the auction please text Charles at 865-851-4774 between the hours of 10am-7pm EST or send us an email.  We do recommend coming to preview as to better judge items for yourself, especially if you have multiple questions.

Any questions about shipping should be directed to our third party shipper, at one of the numbers below, or to our office.  Items for shipping will not be taken to shipper until after pickup is over.  Please give the shipper time to assess your items and they will contact you with an estimate by email.

NOTICE:  Due to an abuse of us transporting items to our shipper, if you pick up your items at the shipper instead of having them shipped, you will be assessed a surcharge.  This will vary depending on the items transported.  Minimal fee will be $10.  Factors in fee will be distance from estate and size or quantity of items taken.  This is to compensate our shipping partner and the person's gas and time transporting the items.  We apologize for any inconvience this may cause.
 
Please bring help to load heavy items and large lots of items.  Also, please note pick up days and times.  These are the only days we will be at the estate.  If you cannot make these times, then please do not bid.  We will not be able to be at the estate other than the days posted for pick up.  Thank you for your cooperation and good luck bidding.

Liberty Pack & Ship
134 S. Main St 
Clinton, TN 37716
865-207-0753
info@libertypackandship.com

Thank you and good luck!

Principal Auctioneer:
Jason Deel TAL 6826
Affiliate Auctioneer:
Jon Dice TAL 7640
Affiliate Auctioneer:
Charles Dailey TAL 7663

title: The Little Sportsman Online Gun, Ammo, & Sporting Auction
description:
The Little Sportsman Online Gun, Ammo, & Sporting Goods Auction

Auction will start to soft close on Saturday Feb. 28th Starting at 6:00pm

Shipping is Available

1. Items need paid for at the time of pick up in the form of Cash, Good PA Check. 
10% buyers preamium for Cash or check, 14% for Cards.
2. If Items are being  picked up in person, they will need picked up on Wednesday March 4th from 11:00am -5:00pm unless shipping as been aranged. 
3. Item Pick up location is at 1153 Susquehanna Trail, Northumberland, PA 17857
4. Any questions you can call The Little Sportsman Shop at 570-473-8383
5. Hours of Pick up are 11:00am - 5:00 PM at  The Little Sportsman Shop at 1153 Susquehanna Trail, Northumberland, PA 17857, Pick up is in basement inside the side doors
6. Sales Tax will be charged to anyone without tax exempt form.  
7. If items need shiped, buyer must call The Little Sportsman Shop by Friday after the auction has ended to arange payment, FFL Info, and shipping info. The phone number to call is 570-473-8383
8. Guns are $50 per gun to ship
Non Gun Items ship at cost
9. Payment is due prior to background check. If you can not pass background check and you purchased firearms than we will resell gun/ammo as a consignment for you.

title: ETX Estate Auction of a Lifetime Over 1,000 Lots It all GOES
description:
Estate Auction of a Lifetime – Over 1,000 Lots – Everything Must Sell!This is the auction you wait all year for. After selling his home, this lifelong collector is parting with everything—tools, Harley-Davidson memorabilia, fishing gear, man-cave treasures, furniture, collectibles, and decades of carefully accumulated equipment. Whether you're a mechanic, outdoorsman, Harley enthusiast, collector, reseller, or simply love finding one-of-a-kind pieces, this sale delivers opportunity in every lot. From shop tools and garage essentials to décor, furnishings, and rare finds, there is something for everyone—and with over 1,000 lots, many items will sell at true auction value. Don’t miss your chance to uncover hidden gems, stock your shop, furnish your home, or score incredible deals. Every item tells a story, and every bid puts you one step closer to owning it. Bid early, bid often, and don’t let someone else take home your next great find.

title: CGC, Comics, Cards & Collectibles! Estate Auction Part 2
description:
This auction showcases the second installment of a long time Detroit area comic shop owner’s personal collection, with keys, graded comics, as well as runs, sets, groups and singles from Marvel, DC, Valiant, and more. From modern mega-keys like Amazing Spider-Man #300 and New Mutants #98 graded by CGC to Bronze and Copper Age favorites, collectors will find first appearances, classic McFarlane covers, and complete runs that rarely surface together. Beyond comics, the auction dives deep into 1990s nostalgia with an outstanding Simpsons World of Springfield figure run, ToyFare exclusives, interactive environments, and Treehouse of Horror sets, plus Dragon Ball Z, Evangelion, Star Wars, and horror toys, many factory sealed. Sports and pop culture fans will find vintage starter hats, signed memorabilia, trading cards, and POGs alongside cult favorites like The Munsters, Get Smart, and classic anime posters.

title: Dear Diamonds And Jewelry Auction Ends 02/28/2026
description:
READ BELOW - Important Auction Information

HOW DOES THIS WORK? It's simple. Dear Diamonds and Jewelry is an online public auction company specializing in jewelry, antique coins and other rarities. These items are offered for sale to the public on our website for a fixed period of time with a defined closing date and time. To register you simply sign up for an account on our secure website. Your information is validated and then you may begin placing your bids!

HOW DO I REGISTER? At the top of your screen, click "Login/New Bidder." If you are a new bidder, click the black box. This will prompt you to enter your email address. You will be emailed a link to validate your email, and then you can finish your registration. It's fast, easy and secure. The whole process takes about 2 minutes. Your credit card will be validated by and in doing so, a temporary hold of $1 will be placed on your account to ensure the credit card is valid, and that only legitimate bidders are participating. This process is exactly like paying for gas at the pump! The charge will fall off of your account in 24-48 hours.

HOW DOES THE AUCTION WORK? The auction is open for bidding as soon as it goes "LIVE" online. Bidding is super easy, at any time you can begin placing your bids and may enter a maximum bid.

title: Niles, MI Online Auction--Lexus RX330 SUV, Gold +
description:
Don't miss this beautifully curated living estate auction featuring a 2004 Lexus RX330 AWD with only 89k miles, clean title, and service records are available. 

This sale includes over 550 lots of high quality items, including fine jewelry such as 14k and 18k gold pieces, jade, sterling silver, vintage brooches, and collectible brands like Eisenberg and Trifari. 
Furniture highlights include Drexel, Hickory, an Ethan Allen leather couch, Thomasville tables, French Provincial and Chippendale pieces, an MCM swivel bookcase, and a Brunswick Brentwood pool table. 

Art offerings range from paintings and lithographs to pottery, bronze tiles, and decorative glass and Chinoiserie pieces. The collection also includes 150+ books-leather bound classics, antique editions, and works by Twain, Melville, Dickens, and more. 

Housewares feature Le Creuset, Pyrex, Corning Ware, Waterford, Fenton, Longaberger baskets, and useful appliances. Clothing includes Pendleton coats, leather and bomber jackets, cashmere, vintage purses, and brands like Coach and Dooney & Bourke. 

Additional items include cameras (Nikon, Minolta, Polaroid), antique tools, a Schwinn recumbent bike, glass insulators, and a 1976 Bicentennial flag. 

This auction begins to close Saturday, February 28, 2026, at 7:00pm EST, with pickup on Sunday, March 1, 2026 from 10:00am-3:00pm EST on-sight at the River Road location in Niles, MI. Winning bidders will receive the exact property address on the emailed receipt, which you will receive after the auction closes on February 28th. Vehicle pick-up can be arranged on another day. 

Thank you for taking the time to explore this auction and happy bidding. 

Tricia Wainscott, Licensed Auctioneer & Realtor
574-329-1204
Lic#AU11300117

title: Baseball Card Auction
description: RARE VINTAGE SPORTS CARD COLLECTION: 1950's -1989 HALL OF FAME ROOKIES & LEGENDS Collectors, this is the auction you've been waiting for! We're thrilled to present an exceptional collection of vintage football and baseball cards spanning nearly two decades of sports history—from the golden age of 1969 through the iconic 1980s era. This isn't just another card lot; this is your opportunity to own pieces of sporting greatness.THE CROWN JEWELS:Leading this remarkable collection are some of the most sought-after rookie cards from the 1980s boom era. Featured prominently are Don Mattingly's legendary 1984 Topps, Fleer, and Donruss rookie cards—the Yankees captain whose cards define an entire generation of collectors. These aren't just cardboard; they're memories of pinstripes, Gold Gloves, and pure baseball excellence.But that's just the beginning! This collection boasts an incredible lineup of Hall of Fame rookie cards including Tony Gwynn (Mr. Padre himself with that sweet .338 career average), Wade Boggs (the hitting machine), Kirby Puckett (Minnesota's beloved icon), Ryne Sandberg (Ryno!), Roger Clemens (The Rocket with 7 Cy Youngs), and even dual-sport legend Bo Jackson. Every single one of these players earned their place in Cooperstown, and now their rookies can earn a place in your collection!FOOTBALL LEGENDS:The gridiron is well-represented with absolute gems from the 1970s and 80s. We're talking Steve Largent, Lynn Swann, Joe Montana, Steve Young, Walter Payton, Roger Staubach, and more. These aren't just cards—they're snapshots of Super Bowl glory, record-breaking performances, and NFL immortality.WHAT MAKES THIS COLLECTION SPECIAL:Every card in this auction is in Near Mint to Excellent condition—carefully preserved and ready to grade or add straight to your collection. We're talking clean corners, sharp edges, and vibrant colors that transport you back to the day these legends first stepped onto the field. No creases, no tears, just quality vintage cardboard the way it should be.This collection represents the absolute peak of baseball and football card collecting: the late 70s revolution when Topps dominated, through the explosive 80s when Donruss and Fleer entered the game and changed everything.

Contact Beth Williams at 615-922-9223 for appointment.

No Returns: All sales are final. There are no returns, refunds, or adjustments.
All bidders must meet all State & Federal Firearm requirements and be of legal age to bid on firearms and ammunition.
American Realty and Auction LLC will handle all paperwork, and gun transfers as well as packaging for shipping. A $30 transfer fee for 1st gun then $15 for each additional gun. Out of state buyers, please note all guns and firearms must be transferred to an authorized Federal Firearms Licensed gun dealer in your area, all handling, packaging & shipping fees are the buyer's responsibility. Handling and packaging will be a minimum of $25 per gun plus shipping fees. 
All Firearms purchased must be made by the same person that is completing the 4473 back ground check form. 4473 Back Ground with TICS must be processed and approved before any firearm can be transferred to the Buyer. If you can not complete a 4473 Back Ground check with TICS then please do not bid. No Refunds will be given if Back Ground Check is Processed and denied. If Back ground check is Denied the buyer has 7 days to file appeal paper work with the TICS for further investigation. If paper work is not filed with the 7-day time period the Buyer forfeits the right to the firearms and no refund will be given. If paper work has been filled American Realty and Auction will work with the buyer during the process to help in any way TCIS needs. If TCIS appeal comes back as denied then firearms will not be transferred and no refund will be given. 
Transfers and Shipping is available for Firearms. All Firearms have to be transferred and shipped to an authorized FFL Dealer. ALL FFL DEALER INFORMATION (including a copy of their FFL) IS REQUIRED TO BE SENT TO AMERICAN REALTY AND AUCTION WITHIN 7 DAYS OF AUCTION. It is the buyer's reasonability to get this information to American Realty and Auction.  No Refunds will be given if FFL information is not received and firearm is unable to be transferred; Firearms will then belong to American Realty and Auction.
All Items must be paid for within one week of auction completion, NO EXCEPTIONS! If gun/guns are not picked up during scheduled pickup times you will need to pick up by appointment only, not to exceed a week after auction close. For pickup appointment contact Beth Williams at 615-922-9223. 
Buyer's Premium: A buyer's premium of 10% bid will be added to the final bid to determine the final sales price for the auction lot.

Auctioneer Drew Williams at 615-504-0632
All items sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with all faults and with all added qualities not listed as well. 
All descriptions believed accurate but not warranted.

title: Sportscard Auction Day 2
description: RARE VINTAGE SPORTS CARD COLLECTION: 1950's -1989 HALL OF FAME ROOKIES & LEGENDS Collectors, this is the auction you've been waiting for! We're thrilled to present an exceptional collection of vintage football and baseball cards spanning nearly two decades of sports history—from the golden age of 1969 through the iconic 1980s era. This isn't just another card lot; this is your opportunity to own pieces of sporting greatness.THE CROWN JEWELS:Leading this remarkable collection are some of the most sought-after rookie cards from the 1980s boom era. Featured prominently are Don Mattingly's legendary 1984 Topps, Fleer, and Donruss rookie cards—the Yankees captain whose cards define an entire generation of collectors. These aren't just cardboard; they're memories of pinstripes, Gold Gloves, and pure baseball excellence.But that's just the beginning! This collection boasts an incredible lineup of Hall of Fame rookie cards including Tony Gwynn (Mr. Padre himself with that sweet .338 career average), Wade Boggs (the hitting machine), Kirby Puckett (Minnesota's beloved icon), Ryne Sandberg (Ryno!), Roger Clemens (The Rocket with 7 Cy Youngs), and even dual-sport legend Bo Jackson. Every single one of these players earned their place in Cooperstown, and now their rookies can earn a place in your collection!FOOTBALL LEGENDS:The gridiron is well-represented with absolute gems from the 1970s and 80s. We're talking Steve Largent, Lynn Swann, Joe Montana, Steve Young, Walter Payton, Roger Staubach, and more. These aren't just cards—they're snapshots of Super Bowl glory, record-breaking performances, and NFL immortality.WHAT MAKES THIS COLLECTION SPECIAL:Every card in this auction is in Near Mint to Excellent condition—carefully preserved and ready to grade or add straight to your collection. We're talking clean corners, sharp edges, and vibrant colors that transport you back to the day these legends first stepped onto the field. No creases, no tears, just quality vintage cardboard the way it should be.This collection represents the absolute peak of baseball and football card collecting: the late 70s revolution when Topps dominated, through the explosive 80s when Donruss and Fleer entered the game and changed everything.

title: ESTATE AUCTION 3-1-26 PART 2 ~ PICKERS PARADISE
description:
?? Estate Auction – Part 2: Eclectic Treasures from a Lifetime Collection! ??

This week's continuation of the estate auction in Allen, Texas, is loaded with antiques, vintage collectibles, mid-century pieces, primitives, toys, cookware, jewelry, and unique oddities. A true mixed haul from one estate—perfect for collectors, decorators, and treasure hunters!
Key Highlights:

Striking Mirrors, Lamps & Lighting, Gold Gilt Eagle-Accented Convex Mirror (39.5 x 24"), Antique Gas Lamp with Green Stained Glass, Opalescent & Porcelain Table Lamps (floral/gold detailing), Tall Blue MCM Glass Table Lamp, Vintage Wall Sconces, Ornate Brass Two-Arm Wall Sconce, and more decorative fixtures.

Vintage Toys & Childhood Nostalgia Shirley Temple 13-Inch Doll with original book, Fisher-Price "My Friend" Becky & Jenny Dolls in original boxes, LEGO Star Wars 2-in-1 Battle Pack, Hot Wheels in original packaging (including 90s editions), Tom Thumb Toy Cash Register (aqua blue), Magnastiks Magnetic Toy Set, Tin Wind-Up Toys, Atari Paddle Controllers with games (Pac-Man & more), and Shirley Temple memorabilia lot.

Posters & Pop Culture Large Vintage Harris Comics Vampirella Poster (72×24"), Signed Elvira Poster (22×17) by One Stop Posters—great finds for fans!

Antiques, Primitives & Tools Primitive Handcrafted Covered Wagon Mailbox, Cast Iron Door Stop (ship shape), 2-Sided Cast Iron Fish & Sea Life Mold Pan, Antique Wooden Barometer, Cast Iron Fry Pot & Self-Basting Lid, Splitting Maul, 48-Inch Pittsburgh Farm Jack (7000 lb capacity), Heavy Auger Bit, Vintage Industrial Lamps & Signs (DeVilbiss, RAB Hazardous Location), large lot of Vintage Letterpress Printing Blocks (wood & metal—typography, portraits, horse motifs, logos—ideal for crafters or history buffs), and more primitives.

Porcelain, China & Figurines, Hummel Figurine (child on skis), Staffordshire & Aynsley Bone China floral pieces, Capodimonte "Spring Flowers" Basket, Lenox Porcelain Heart-Design Vase & Bowl Set, Royal Adderley arrangements, collectible thimbles (wildlife/floral), and assorted ceramic figurines.
Cookware & Kitchen Gems Vintage Magnalite Aluminum Set (Dutch ovens, chicken fryer, saucepans with lids), KitchenAid Classic Stand Mixer (stainless bowl), Cuisinart Pot with Strainer, and survival food lots (Provident Pantry, Mountain House).

Jewelry, Decor & Oddities Vintage & Costume Jewelry (sterling silver, brooches, elephant themes, enamel designs), Religious Necklaces & Crosses, Tintypes & Cabinet Cards (late 19th-century portraits & "Instant Ancestors" lots), Large Wooden Chest (40×25.5×19"), Tall Wooden Corner Display Cabinet, Tufted Blue Velvet Bench, Vintage Microscope in case, Astatic Microphone, Bayonet, Air Rifle, Bolt-Action Rifle, and tons more—including survival items, seeds, and quirky collectibles!

Many items in original boxes or with provenance—true estate freshness!

Tag a friend who loves vintage & antiques! Questions? See you bidding! 

#EstateAuction #VintageFinds #Antiques #Collectibles #AllenTX*

title: Investing in Bright Futures: The Apogee Gulf Coast Auction
description:
Apogee Gulf Coast Gala Online Auction — Now Live

Welcome to the official Apogee Gulf Coast Gala Online Auction.

This auction is now live and open for participation as we celebrate our community and raise support for Apogee Gulf Coast. You’re invited to explore a thoughtfully curated collection of auction items and raffle previews as part of our upcoming Gala.

*****
WHAT TO KNOW AS YOU BROWSE:
--- Online bidding is now open for eligible auction items
--- Some sections include placeholder listings designed to explain how the auction and raffle function
--- Raffle preview listings are not available for online bidding and are clearly marked
--- Raffle tickets are sold in person at the Gala only, and guests must be present to win
--- Items, images, and descriptions may continue to be refined as we finalize details leading up to the event


*****
WHY YOU’LL SEE PLACEHOLDERS & PREVIEWS: 
These listings are intentionally included to help guests:
 --- Understand how the auction and raffle are structured
--- Preview raffle prizes ahead of time
--- Easily navigate which items are available for online bidding vs. in-person raffles
--- Plan ahead for the Gala experience


*****
FEEDBACK & QUESTIONS:
We’ve worked hard to make this auction clear, engaging, and easy to navigate.

If you have feedback, questions, or notice anything that could improve the experience, we’d love to hear from you: info@texasauctions.biz


*****
EVENT DETAILS:
All proceeds support the Apogee Gulf Coast Gala on February 28, 2026, benefiting: 
--- Student scholarships
--- Campus growth
--- Hands-on, learner-driven education programs

Join us! Purchase tickets below:

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd985DEm1anyLJk2I9Wizk_78pzJmrhyWt0waBSBqhLut0hEQ/viewform


Thank you for being part of this fundraiser and for supporting the Apogee Gulf Coast community. We’re excited to celebrate with you and make this a meaningful night for our learners.

title: Premier Collectibles, Automobilia, Rock & Roll,Sports Cards
description:
Premier Midwestern Estate Auction: Rare Collectibles & Indiana Historical TreasureDiscover an extraordinary collection featuring over 500 lots of premium vintage collectibles, rare memorabilia, and a remarkable piece of Indiana history—

***The Catherine Winters antique German bisque doll collection. Nine-year-old Catherine Winters disappeared from New Castle, Indiana on March 20, 1913. Her father preserved her cherished doll hoping for her safe return. This early 20th-century bisque head doll with brown sleeping eyes, leather body, and original pale yellow dress with purple ribbon trim comes with wheeled carriage, news articles, videos, and purchase receipt—a true piece of Indiana history.

Premium Highlights:Vintage Sports Cards & Memorabilia: Graded Mickey Mantle 1964 Topps #50 & 1975 cards, Hank Aaron 1965 & 1972 Topps in acrylic slabs, LeBron James 2003-04 Upper Deck Top Prospects Promo rookie card graded Gem Mint 10, George Brett 1975 Topps #228, Willie Mays 1973 Topps, O.J. Simpson 1970 Topps, Babe Ruth & Ted Williams 1960 Fleer, autographed cards with game-worn jersey pieces including Michael Jordan, Magic Johnson, Larry Bird Starting Lineup figures, Dale Earnhardt commemorative collections, Peyton Manning & Colts collectibles

Rare Barbie Dolls & Accessories: 1962 Brunette Bubble Cut Barbie dolls (one wearing Cruise Stripes #918, other in red Helenca swimsuit), 1965 Mattel American Girl Barbie #1070, 1964 Miss Barbie #1060, vintage 1961-1967 Ken dolls including 1962 Ken with Red Vest fashion ensemble #1411, 1964 Allen dolls, vintage Midge dolls in original coral swimsuits, Bob Mackie Gold Barbie (1990 limited edition), Bob Mackie Empress Bride Barbie with original box and poster, 1995 Holiday Jewel Porcelain Barbie with receipt, Set of 3 Barbie Fashion Classic Series Coca-Cola dolls in original boxes (11.5" high), Barbie Loves Elvis Collector Edition Gift Set, 1994 Barbie as Dorothy from Wizard of Oz (Hollywood Legends Collection), "Nifty 50's" Barbie from Great Fashions of 20th Century series, vintage 1962 Mattel Barbie Austin-Healey convertible (first car ever produced for Barbie), 1996 Barbie Convertible Car Clock Radio, Hot Wheels Barbie Highway Hauler diecast truck new in package, Barbie comics, watch, charm bracelet, vintage 1964 Barbie cases with accessories, 2006 Genevieve Ballerina Barbie, 2005 Holiday Barbie by Bob Mackie, New Barbie The 12 Dancing Princesses Interactive Princess Genevieve doll

Antique Dolls & Porcelain Collectibles: Royal Dux porcelain "Diana the Huntress" sculpture with Borzoi wolfhound (14.25" high), Royal Dux seated and reclining figures, Madame Alexander collectibles including Welcome Home 1991, Sleeping Beauty, ballerina dolls, Cleopatra Portrait of History, German bisque dolls by Heinrich Handwerck (24" & 29" high) with sleepy eyes and articulated joints, Armand Marseille Bisque "Dream Baby," antique German bisque "American School Boy" character doll, 1950s-60s Vogue Ginny walker dolls, vintage Effanbee 1984 Judy Garland Dorothy doll, 15-inch American Character "Tiny Tears" doll circa 1950s with case, limited edition Disney porcelain dolls: Cinderella & Prince Charming set, Cinderella Fairy Godmother, Lady Tremaine, Anastasia & Drizella, Snow White, Belle Beauty and the Beast (all with Certificates of Authenticity), Annette Funicello "Tiara" limited edition mohair collectible bear

Rock & Roll Memorabilia: The Beatles complete collection including White Album with 2 LPs and poster insert, Abbey Road, Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band, Revolver, Hard Day's Night, Beatles VI, Introducing The Beatles (1964), Beatles '65, Yellow Submarine figures in packages, framed concert poster from Indianapolis State Fairgrounds, vintage 1964 Worcester Ware Beatles metal serving tray, Beatles cards and photos. Elvis Presley collectibles: 45+ albums and 45 records, Pez dispensers, Fossil limited edition watch, 1987 Memories movie star watch, swinging legs pendulum clock, record-style wall clock, Graceland musical sculpture, Bradford Exchange Blue Christmas Tree, vintage Dakin Special Edition doll (1985), Hasbro '68 Special 12-inch doll, Mattel Collector Edition dolls, commemorative knife sets, Zippo lighters, ornaments. Rolling Stones "Sticky Fingers" album, Jimi Hendrix albums (Axis Bold as Love, Smash Hits with poster), George Harrison 3-record "All Things Must Pass" set, Paul McCartney solo albums

Italian Collectible Cars & Precision Model Racing: Highly detailed 1:12 scale models including Ferrari 512S (1970 Le Mans), 1974 McLaren M23, Lotus Type 49 (Jim Clark 1967 Dutch Grand Prix winner), Tyrrell P34 Six Wheeler, Ferrari 312B (Jacky Ickx 1970 Mexican Grand Prix), McLaren Honda MP4/6 (Ayrton Senna 1991), 1967 Eagle Gurney-Weslake (Dan Gurney Belgian Grand Prix victory), multiple 1:18 scale models: 1965 Chaparral 2, Lamborghini Countach LP400 with acrylic case, 1966 Lola T70 Spyder (Mark Donohue Sunoco Special), Ferrari 250 Testa Rossa, Porsche models.
 Vintage Dinky Toys: military vehicles, Centurion tanks, cars with original boxes. Solido Italian-made Ferrari collection (14 cars), Brumm Ori Series Fiat collectibles

Disney & Wizard of Oz Collectibles: Mickey Mouse vintage doll with stitched features (18" high), Mickey & Minnie figures and clocks, Pinocchio music box and Italian handcrafted puppet, vintage coloring books, Thomas Kinkade puzzles, records (Snow White, Peter Pan), Wizard of Oz purses, books, figures, cards, 1994 Barbie as Dorothy

Comic Book Collection & Big Little Books: Comic Cavalcade #13 (1945 Flash/DC), Katy Keene fashion comics spanning 1949-1961 including rare #1 (1949), vintage Gene Autry comics, Roy Rogers comics from 1940s-50s, Star Trek, Mickey Mouse, Dark Shadows, Archie Comics collection in binders, 25 vintage Big Little Books featuring Mickey Mouse, Shirley Temple, Popeye

Additional Premium Collectibles: James Dean & Marilyn Monroe memorabilia including limited edition dolls and promotional items, Hot Wheels collectibles new in packages, Wedgwood Imperial Porcelain basin & pitcher set, foreign coins & currency from worldwide origins, sterling silver rings and accessories (4.09 oz troy), Hummel figures and Goebel crystal, Coca-Cola porcelain cookie jars and collectibles throughout

Furniture: Oak bookcases, Empire style display cabinets, pie safes, curio cabinets, Victorian sideboard buffets—no shipping available on furniture pieces.

Perfect for serious collectors, investors, and history enthusiasts throughout Illinois, Indiana, Ohio, Michigan, and the greater Midwest region!

title: Antiques, New Cosco Items, Glassware, Coins & More!
description:
Get ready for an unforgettable auction experience! Join us Sunday March 1st @ 2PM as we auction off a treasure trove of Antiques, New Cosco Pallet Items, Glassware, Oil Lamps, Vintage Collectibles , Coins & More! With food prepared by Bonnie, giveaways and more all while enjoying the fun of an all day auction! 

PLEASE READ AUCTION INFO BELOW

ALL ITEMS SOLD AS IS, WHERE IS, HOW IS WITH NO WARRANTIES IMPLIED OR EXPRESSED!
PLEASE TAKE TIME TO EXAMINE ALL ITEMS BEFORE BIDDING!!

We suggest you place your highest bid prior to the auction starting because we move very fast through the lots and you may not get time to increase your bid before the lot closes. 
This is an auction as described as "public auction" under WV Code. All bidding increments, bidding amounts, bidding styles, time limits are strictly at the discretion of the auctioneer. Also "Prebidding Increments" may change during the LIVE sale; MEANING THEY WILL NOT FOLLOW THE PREBIDDING INCREMENTAL BIDDING CHART.

We hold a Federal Firearms License; therefore all firearms will be transferred onsite on day of sale if purchased in person, or can be picked up on Monday from 10-6, or by appointment. Firearms purchased online can be picked up on site as well or can be shipped to the FFL of your choice (Buyer Pays Shipping)

BUYERS must be 21 years of age or older to purchase a pistol, and 18 years of age or older to purchase a long gun. 

No paperwork or requirements on muzzleloaders as per the law. 

There will be a $20.00 one-time fee if a background check is required, but it will be charged only once regardless of how many firearms you purchase. IF you have a WV concealed carry permit there is NO FEE.

ATTENTION ALL ONLINE BIDDERS: THIS IS A SIMULCAST AUCTION MEANING THAT IT IS BOTH ONLINE AND PERSON AT THE SAME TIME. IN THE EVENT OF A TIE BID ONLINE THE BID WILL GO TO THE EARLIEST PLACED BID, BUT THAT DOES NOT APPLY ONCE THE LIVE AUCTION STARTS IN REGARDS TO FLOOR BIDDERS. IF THE AUCTIONEER TAKES A BID ON THE FLOOR FOR YOUR MAX BID AMOUNT BEFORE IT IS REACHED THE BID WILL GO TO THE FLOOR. ALL ONLINE BIDDERS MUST PAY VIA THE CC ON FILE UNLESS PRIOR ARRANGEMENTS ARE MADE WITH AUCTIONEER. 

SHIPPING IS AVAILABLE ON ALL ITEMS. THERE WILL BE A FEE FOR HANDLING AND PACKAGING MATERIALS. BUYER PAYS SHIPPING ON ALL ITEMS. We are expierenced glass shippers and we do all packaging in house and use Pirate Ship to save you the most money possible!

"Oak Summit is the Premier Auction Company of the South Branch Valley of West Virginia"

Online Bidding Available via hibid.com 10%  buyers premium if bought online. No buyers premium if purchased in-person in-house at time of auction. There will be a 10% buyers premium for absentee or phone bids.

Terms of sale are cash or check with proper ID, Debit and Credit Cards will be accepted with a 5% Processing Fee. There will be a 10% buyers premium on all absentee or phone bids. If the auctioneer approves you to pay via Paypal (MUST BE APPROVED BEFORE AUCTION BEGINS there will a 5% fee for processing. ALL ONLINE BIDDERS MUST PAY VIA THE CC ON FILE UNLESS PRIOR ARRANGEMENTS ARE MADE WITH AUCTIONEER. 
Titled items will be released day of sale, but if payment is made by check titles will be held and mailed once the check clears. Announcements on sale day precedence over any advertising. Licensed and bonded in favor of the State of WV Lic #2151
In the event that a piece(s) of glass arrives broken, the buyer must report it to the seller within 48 hours of delivery. The Buyer also must provide the Seller with photos of the box, packing materials, and broken item(s). The seller will file an insurance claim with the postal service, but the buyer must keep the box, packing materials and broken item(s) until the claim is resolved. The seller will communicate any additional needs to the buyer during the claim process. -The seller is not responsible for any items being sent to a drop hub distributor. Buyer must report any damages to seller within 24 hours of item being received at distributor. Buyer is also aware that they must purchase additional insurance from distributor for overseas shipment. Seller will not refund, replace or exchange any item for damage if not notified within 24 hours of delivery to agreed upon location in the US. Seller is not responsible for any issues once the item has left the original box and/or shipment has left the United States. title: Monday March 2nd Fine Jewelry & Luxury Brands Auction
description:
READ BELOW. Important Auction Information

1.) Register to bid. At the top of your screen you will see a button labeled "Login/New Bidder". You will need to click this and fill out the basic information to create your new account or login to your existing account. If you have bid with us in the past and have forgotten your Username or Password, you can reset them from there. There is no charge to register and bid, you only pay if you win an item. The credit card on file will be validated by our software company. This means they will put a "hold" on your credit card for $1 and then reverse that charge within 24 hours. This ensures the card is valid and that you are bidding against only other valid bidders.

2.) Preview the entire catalog early and often. New items are added to the catalog as they come in to the warehouse and can sometimes fall on different pages depending on where they are placed in the building. It isn't unusual for new items to be added just minutes before the auction begins.

3.) Pick the items you would like to bid on and PRE-BID with realistic numbers. We can not stress this enough. Items can be lost in the final seconds due to last second bids, Internet outages, power or site failures. The computer will bid on your behalf up to your maximum amount if some else places a bid.
EXAMPLE A.) You place a bid of $100 for an item. Someone else bids $50 and nobody else bids, you win your item for $55.
EXAMPLE B.) You place a bid of $100 for an item. Someone else bids $100 and nobody else bids, you will still win your item at $100 because it is the first bid.
EXAMPLE C.) You place a bid of $100 for an item. Someone else bids $110 in the final seconds. Bidding extends 2 minutes and you will only have 2 minutes total to bid again.

4.) PLEASE READ the terms and conditions. All bidders are bound by the same terms and conditions for this auction included payment options, pickup times and other things important to the auction. Most questions will be answered on our website, but if you have one that isn't you can click the "Feedback" button at the bottom of your screen and we will help you.

5.) Have fun and save money! Interstate Auction & Realty has revolutionized the way new furniture is being sold with our Internet Platform. Do not under estimate how much money you can save. We have buyers every week from several hundred miles away who cannot believe the savings!

title: Trader Jack Estate 1st  Auction
description:
Trader Jack’s Estate – Online Only Auction

Henry Auction Service is proud to present the Online Only Auction from the Trader Jack’s Estate. This outstanding estate features a unique and hard-to-find collection reflecting a lifetime passion for western heritage, and collecting
Buyers will find a great selection of Western décor, taxidermy mounts, antiques, and vintage collectibles, along with many interesting and one-of-a-kind pieces throughout the auction. This is an excellent opportunity for collectors, decorators, antique enthusiasts, and anyone looking to add authentic western character to their home, cabin, shop, or collection.
Whether you're searching for statement display pieces, rustic décor, or valuable collectibles, this auction offers something for everyone. Be sure to browse the entire catalog so you don’t miss these unique estate items.

title: Jane Ellen Kaegy Trust Home and Farmland
description: We are please the Kaegy's have entrusted us to market their parents home and farmland of which some of it has been in the Kaegy family for over 100 years. The late Max and Jane Ellen Kaegy raised two boys on this farm. Max purchased the Kaegy farm in the sixties, from his Greandparents where he farmed and rasied hogs while working and then retiring form Southwestern Electric Cooperative. Jane Ellen was a homemaker and a mother raising to fine boys, Kevin and Mike. The home farm consist of 112 Acres of which Mike live on about 7 acres and not selling. There will be 5+/- acres offered for sale as Tract 2(survey to be completed after the auction) consisting of a 2 bedroom 1 bath home, loafing shed, barn and 3400 bu grain bin. This property has a new upgraded meter loop, county water and septic and aboutt 3 acres of tillable land. Tract 1 will be 105 +/- acres with about 70 acres tillable. Farming right for 2026 are presently under contract with 1/3 of the crop income going to the new owner. Tract 3 consisting of 50 +/- acres has been in the family for over 100 years and is mostly tillable, located at Dudleyville and Hunter School Rd Greenville. IL. Preview and information will be available from 12:30-2:30 PM Feb 15th or by calling Adam at 618-267-3086

title: Shirley Distlehorst Estate Online Auction- Springfield IL
description:
Shirley Distlehorst Estate Online Auction- Springfield IL
Featuring: 460+ lots of Toys, Antiques, Glassware, Household, & more
Address: 41 Belhaven Dr., Springfield IL 62711

Auction Ends: Monday March 2nd at 7:00 PM CST (Soft Close)

In-Person Preview: Thursday February 26th 4pm - 6pm
41 Belhaven Dr., Springfield IL 62711

Item Pick-Up: Wednesday March 4th, 2:00pm - 6:00pm
Pick-up location: 41 Belhaven Dr., Springfield IL 62711

Pickup appointments must be scheduled via Shipping Saint 
(email/text link provided after auction ends).

Ben Ladage Auctions - Auburn, IL
Office: 217-652-0924
View full catalog and bid at: www.benladage.com
Please read the complete Terms & Conditions before placing any bids.
________________________________________
BUYER'S PREMIUM & PAYMENT:

A 15% buyer's premium will be added to each item.
We offer a 3% discount for cash, check, or non-credit card payment - you must notify us before the auction closes to receive this discount.

Accepted payment forms: Visa, Mastercard, Discover, debit card, cash, or approved checks.
No checks accepted without prior approval or established business history. Checks with bank letter of credit may be accepted-please call our office in advance.

Invoices for items will be billed to your registered credit card immediately following the auction. If shipping is selected, you will receive a separate invoice once shipping costs are calculated.
________________________________________
FOLLOWING THE AUCTION:

Winning bidders will receive both an email and text message with the option to select pickup or shipping.
o If pickup is selected, you'll receive full pickup instructions, including date, time, and location based on the information you provided during registration.
o If shipping is selected, your items will be securely packed by our in-house shipping department. Once packaging is complete, you will receive another email and text message with the total shipping cost and handling fee.
________________________________________
SHIPPING:

Shipping is available on most items and provided in-house by Ben Ladage Auctions using Shipping Saint. After the auction:
"	You will receive an email and text to choose either shipping or pick-up.
"	If shipping is selected, items will be packaged, and you will receive a second notification with shipping and handling costs.
"	We use USPS, UPS, and FedEx and always select the most cost-effective method.
"	Please allow up to 15 business days for items to ship after the pickup date, although most packages go out within 5 business days.
We do not ship large or heavy items unless pre-approved prior to auction closing. We do not accept outside shipping labels or provide shipping quotes. Shipping costs are the responsibility of the buyer and are non-refundable.
________________________________________
ITEM DESCRIPTIONS, INSPECTIONS, & DISCLAIMERS:

All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no warranties, guarantees, refunds, or returns of any kind. Descriptions including condition, age, origin, or rarity are offered as opinion only. The bidder assumes all responsibility for determining value and authenticity.
Preview and inspection are strongly encouraged. The auctioneer is not responsible for errors in descriptions or missed bids (online, absentee, phone, or otherwise). All announcements on auction day take precedence over any prior marketing.
________________________________________
PICKUP POLICY - READ CAREFULLY:

All items must be picked up on the scheduled date and time. If you cannot make the scheduled pickup, please do not bid.
Items not picked up are forfeited and become property of Ben Ladage Auctions unless other arrangements have been made in advance.
We understand life happens and will try to work with you-but communication is key. If we have to retrieve your items from the auction site and transport them back to Auburn, a minimum fee of $25 per item will be charged. This fee helps cover time, labor, and tracking logistics.
________________________________________
ABSENTEE BIDDING & TECHNICAL INFORMATION:

Don't want to watch the auction live? The best strategy is to leave your maximum bid ahead of time. Our system will bid incrementally on your behalf.
This is a soft close auction. If a bid is placed in the last 3 minutes of a lot's closing, that lot will automatically extend by 3 minutes. All lots close in staggered intervals.
If you're unsure how to bid online, contact our office before the final day-we're happy to help! Absentee bids are accepted and will include the 15% buyer's premium.
________________________________________

Sellers: Hans Distlehorst

We appreciate your bids and support-thank you for participating in this online auction!

title: MON WKLY #1 JEWELRY COINS LOTS OF SILVER TOYS COLLECTIBLES+

RECEIPT OF ITEMS AND SIGNING OF INVOICE.

IN HOME ESTATES DO NOT HAVE THIS ABILITY AND THEREFORE YOU ARE BIDDING ON AN ITEM BASED ON THE PICTURES AND DESCRIPTION GIVEN KNOWING THAT AN ITEM IF NOT LISTED AS NEW MAY HAVE WEAR, TEAR, OR OTHER POSSIBLE DEFECTS. ULTIMATELY I AM THE FINAL SAY ON THE MATTER AND RESERVE THAT RIGHT.

IF YOU ARE BIDDING AND REQUIRE SHIPPING AND YOU DO NOT HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  YOU ARE BIDDING ON THE ITEM IN A BIT OF A BLIND FORMAT. AGAIN IN THIS CASE YOU ARE RECOGNIZING THAT UNLESS AN ITEM IS DESCRIBED AS NEW THEN THERE MAY BE INTERNAL EXTERNAL FLAWS AND SIGNS OF USAGE AND YOU ARE BIDDING ON THESE ITEMS AS IS WHERE IS NO WARRANTIES ARE EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.

title: Rare Coin & Currency Auction – CC Silver Dollars, Gold
description:
Rare Coin & Currency Auction - Carson City Silver Dollars, Gold & National Bank Notes

This auction features an exceptional lineup of key date U.S. coins, GSA Carson City Morgan Silver Dollars, gold, bullion, and rare national bank notes from Minnesota and Wisconsin. Collectors will find a strong selection of better-date silver dollars, early type coins, and desirable large-size and national currency.

Featured Coin Highlights
1799 Draped Bust Silver Dollar - PCGS VF20
1889-CC Morgan Silver Dollar
1893-CC Morgan Silver Dollar
1903-S Morgan Silver Dollar
1885-CC Morgan Silver Dollar - low mintage Carson City issue
1895-S Morgan Silver Dollar
1909-S VDB Lincoln Cent - PCGS VF20
Rare 1937-d Pcgs Vf35 3-Legged Buffalo Nickel (CAC)
1921 High Relief Peace Dollars
Early Bust, Seated Liberty, Barber, and Standing Liberty type coins

GSA Carson City Morgan Collection
Strong selection of GSA Hoard Morgan Silver Dollars including 1882-CC, 1883-CC, 1884-CC, and 1885-CC issues. Many include original boxes and paperwork, with select graded examples and original GSA packaging. Carson City silver continues to draw strong collector demand.

Gold & Bullion
2016-W Gold Mercury Dime - PCGS SP70 First Strike
Gold Eagles (graded and proof)
5 oz .999 fine silver bars
American Silver Eagles including early and modern issues
Morgan Silver Dollar rolls and better-date singles

Rare Currency & National Bank Notes
Large offering of Minnesota and Wisconsin National Currency Notes including Winona, Rochester, Harmony, Mankato, Eau Claire, Wausau, and Minneapolis issues. Additional highlights include a 1922 Large Size $20 Gold Certificate, 1899 Black Eagle Silver Certificate, 1899 Treasury Note filled star, 1914 large size Federal Reserve Note, Confederate currency and bonds, and other high-grade obsolete and national notes.

This is a premium collector auction with strong depth in Carson City silver, gold, key date U.S. coins, and rare regional currency.

Auction Information
Starts Closing: Monday, March 2nd at 7:00 p.m.

Inspection: By Appointment
You are encouraged to schedule an inspection time prior to bidding.

Pick-Up: Tuesday, March 3rd, 9:00 a.m. - 5:00 p.m. Only
Location:
Becker Auction
504 Bluffview Road
Rushford, MN 55971
Shipping Available. Fees Apply.
All items sold as-is. No refunds or returns. Buyers are responsible for inspecting items for completeness, authenticity, and condition prior to bidding.

title: March Winter Estate Finds
description:
Our March Winter Estate Finds Auction features over 300 lots of artwork, jewelry, furniture, books, silver, and decorative and ethnographic items. 
There is a large selection of Art Deco furniture, decorative accents, and textiles including Chinese Art Deco rugs. Other furniture highlights include a LaVerne L’Etreinte Mirror and Les Chinois Cocktail Table. 
Collectors of Folk Art will find Hooked Rugs in a variety of patterns and an early American School Oil on Canvas. The sale also features a selection of Mid-Century advertising and pop culture memorabilia. Other highlights include ethnographic items such as a Native American Bow and Arrow and 2 Native American Hide War Shirts. 
Jewelry highlights include a 18k & Coral Beaded Necklace, a 14k Gold Nugget Bracelet, and a variety of sterling and beaded pieces. A large selection of books is also featured, with first editions and rare books, including 12 volumes of Freud’s Imago and 6 volumes of his Journal of Psychoanalysis.
