title: Vintage Toys, Cast Iron Banks & Classic Collectibles
description:
This online auction features 376 cataloged lots of cast iron banks, vintage toys, dolls, plush collectibles, games, and children’s books.

Highlights include cast iron mechanical and still banks such as Jonah and the Whale, bull dog and cat banks, along with additional coin banks. Toy offerings include Hubley cast iron toys, Nylint pressed-steel trucks, wind-up toys from Japan and Marx, pull toys, tin toys, cap guns, diecast vehicles, and character-themed toys including Popeye and Snoopy examples.

The auction includes multiple Cabbage Patch Kids dolls (Coleco, 1984), large Raggedy Ann and Andy dolls and related items, Raikes Bears and Steiff plush animals, as well as additional plush and character toys.

Also included are board games and tabletop games such as Monopoly editions, solitaire games, Spear’s Comical Tivoli, and other marble games.

Children’s books are well represented, including vintage and early editions such as Winnie-the-Pooh (1926), Whitman titles, Platt & Munk editions, and grouped book sets.

This is a diverse multi-category collection featuring cast iron banks, pressed steel and tin toys, dolls, plush, character toys, games, and vintage children’s literature.

title: Alice March 12th Auction w/ Pflugerville Transfer Avail.
description:
Welcome to our March 12th Alice (Greater Corpus Area) auction with transfer to Pflugerville available!

We have approx 700 items from a major online retailer and the usual mix of unique items from our consignors! 

All lots start at $5 with no reserve!

ONLINE ONLY - ONLINE ONLY - ONLINE ONLY

Transfer to the Pflugerville warehouse is available on almost all items for an extra 10% added to your buyer's premium. Pallet lots are not eligible for transfer to Pflugerville. See lot details.

ALL INVOICES MUST BE PAID BY 3PM ON SATURDAY MARCH 14TH All invoices not paid by 3 PM on Saturday March 14th will be billed to your card on file.

You will be able to pay your invoice in person (cash or credit card) or an online payment link will be available upon request - simply reply to the invoice email you will receive after the auction and let us know if you will be picking up in Alice or in Pflugerville.

All invoice requiring transfer to Pflugerville must be paid by 3 PM March 14th or will not ship. 

Contact Steve via email at steve@atxauctions.com with any questions.

This auction is online only and will take place on Thursday March 14th. Lots will start to close at 1:15 PM on the 14th and will close at a rate of approx 4 per minute until all lots are sold.

Pickup in Alice is located at our warehouse:

36 S Johnson, 
Alice, TX  78332

on Friday March 15th from 10 AM to 6PM & Saturday March 16th from 10 AM to 4 PM.

Delivery to the Pflugerville location is offered for this auction on most lots. Pallet lots are not eligible for shipping to Pflugerville - see lot details. Pick up in Pflugerville is scheduled for the following Friday March 21st from 10 am to 6 pm and Saturday the 22nd from 10AM to 5 PM - at our location in Pflugerville - 309 North Railroad in the Mokan Storage facility. Alternate pickup times for Pflugerville can be arranged but must be coordinated in advance.

title: Ousley Farms Retirement - Equipment Auction
description:
Welcome to the Ousley Farms Multi Auction Retirement! A total of 3 auctions to complete this entire farm retirement. Here you are at Auction # 2. Details below. 

Auction # 2 is LIVE In-Person and Online on Thursday, March 12th @ 10 AM EST

Location: 5705 W 1300 N Elwood, IN 46036

Open House for Real Estate, Farm Equipment, & Personal Property: Sunday, March 1st from 1 PM to 5 PM

This auction will include all of the Farm Equipment. Don't miss out on this full lineup of an entire farming operation! 
John Deere Tractors inlcuding a 2014 JD 5065E, 2016 JD 6120R, 2007 JD 7420, 1989 JD 4755, and a 2004 JD 9220. A 2016 John Deere 324K Loader. Excellent lineup of Hay Equipment including a 2022 New Holland BC5070 Square Baler, 2020 JD 450M Round Baler, 2020 JD 630 MoCo, Kuhn Speedrake SR 110 GII, Kuhn GF 5202 Hay Tedder, Out Front Accumulator, and grapple, JD 265 Disc Mower, 2020 JD R280 Disc Mower, and 6 hay wagons! Planting Equipment from a 2003 JD 1690 Soybean Special Drill, Sunflower 9421-30 Grain Drill, JD 1760 12 Row Conservation Planter, and a JD 7000 6 Row Planter. Harvest Equipment including a 2006 JD 9760 STS Combine, 2000 JD 9650 STS Combine, JD 635F Hydraflex Grain Platform, JD 930F Grain Platform, 2008 JD 612C Corn Head, 3 Head Carts, a Demco 850 Grain cart, 4 Gravity Wagons, 2 grain trucks, a semi and a grain trailer. A 2011 Walinga Agri-Vac 6614. A 2022 Chevrolet Silverado 1500, 2007 Regal Fleetwood Exreme Edition Camper, 2022 Forest River Salem Camper, 2016 John Deere 825i Gator, 2008 Trailer Man Gooseneck Trailer, 2018 Load Trail 14ft Dump Trailer, 2011 Trailerman 20ft Tilt Trailer, 2 John Deere 980 field cultivators, Unverferth 1225 28ft Double Basket Rolling Harrow, DMI 26ft Crumbler, 2 DMI 13 Knife Anhydrous Applicators, 2 Patriot 100 Seed Tenders, a 3 box seed tender, 1977 Ford F350 Flatbed Truck, Krause 1900 25ft Disc, John Deere Z960R 60in Mower, John Deere X390 54in Riding Mower, and more!

For Auction 2 - NO Buyers Premium for In-Person bidders! Save money by attending this auction on-site!
Buyers premium varies for online platforms. Use BidBoone.com for 5% BP with $2,500 cap. Use Equipment Facts or HiBid platforms for 7% BP with $7,500 cap.There is no sales tax on this portion of the retirement auction!


Auction # 1 will include two Online Only Auctions for the personal property of this retirement.

Online Only Day 1 is on Wednesday, March 4th @ 5 PM. This auction will include tools, shop items, farm supplies, Matco, Craftsman, John Deere, Dewalt, Ryobi, Pittsburg tools, and more! Wrenches, Sockets, Plyers, Screwdrivers, Tool Boxes, Chainsaws, Bolt Bins, and much more! 

Online Only Day 2 is on Thursday, March 5th @ 5 PM. This auction will include Collectible Coins, Silver Coins, Jewelry, McCoy Pottery, Roseville Pottery, Furniture, Antiques, Precious Moments, Loads of Decorations, Milk Bottles, Huge Beer Can Collection, Vera Bradley, and more!

Auction # 3 - LIVE In-Person and Online on Thursday, March 12th @ Approx. 1 PM EST. 
This auction will follow the conclusion of the Farm Equipment. - An exceptional offering of Real Estate. A total of 80 +/- acres being offered in two tracts.

Tract 1 - 10 +/- Acres with a full list of great features including the 3 Bedroom / 2 Bathroom 2,092 sqft home! Downstairs is over a 1,000 sqft basement that was used for storage, utilities, and extra space! Large primary bedroom with walk in closet and bathroom. Spacious family room just off the kitchen. Stainless appliances with a newer LP Gas Stove and a newer microwave. Spare dining room and an office space just outside 2 other bedrooms. Multiple new upgrades! Within last two years this home has had all new windows and a new roof! The water softener is owned and was upgraded to a quality system in last few years. New vinyl plank flooring throughout part of the house. New carpet in the primary bedroom. The list goes on! The home has a detached 2+ car garage with a large open porch that was used for relaxing and seeing over the large yard and firepit area.

Tract 1 comes with over 80,000 bushels of grain storage! 4 Brock Grain Bins and an additional 2,000 bushel holding bin. The bins are 40,000 Bushels, 20,000 Bushels,12,000 Bushels, and 12,000 Bushels. All 4 bins have air tubes on them currently. There is no shortage of farm storage on this property as well! 2 Morton Barns - 1 has a 36x54 heated shop and 54x63 storage area. The second Morton Barn measures 69x71 - Featuring a loading dock setup and a 17x26 insulated vegetable cooler! There is also a 29x45 Pole Barn and a 24x29 block building.

Tract 2 - 70 +/- Acres of mostly tillable farmland! Approximately 62 acres of tillable ground. The other 8+/- acres is made up of a sparsely wooded pasture space that has been used for cattle and hay over the years. As well as an approximately 2 acre pond. The Ousley's have farmed this ground for the last 50+ years that they have owned it. Wayne said he has never lost a crop on this ground due to standing water. It drains extremely well with the Chalfant Ditch that runs through the entire property. The county just put in an all new outlet on the south end of the farm.

There is 46 acres of wheat planted on Tract 2. The buyer of Tract 2 will have the right to harvest the wheat crop and split the gross vlaue of the wheat sold 50/50 with the seller. The right to bale the leftover straw has been assigned. The buyer of Tract 2 will receive $2,000 after the straw has been baled from the seller. Once the wheat and straw have been removed the buyer will have full access to that part of the farmground to do as they want. The buyer will have possession of the other tillable ground that has not been planted at closing. 

Reach out with any questions or to schedule a time to view the real estate!Caleb Cleek (765)635-9861

description:
Online Toy Train, Sports Card & Collectibles Auction

Closes: Thursday, March 12th starting at 6:00 PM
Pickup: Friday, March 13th | 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
Preview Location: Walton Auction Co. Office
227 W. Wyandot Ave., Upper Sandusky, OH

Walton Auction Co. is excited to present an outstanding collection of vintage Lionel, American Flyer, Williams, Hafner and Kusan O-Gauge trains, along with a large assortment of sports card sets, memorabilia, and collectibles.

This sale features a strong lineup of post-war Lionel steam locomotives, pre-war pieces, rare accessories, and hard-to-find engines, plus sealed baseball card sets from the late 1980s and early 1990s.

Featured Train Highlights:
•	Lionel 736 Berkshire Steam Engine with 2466 coal car
•	Lionel 675 & 736 Steam Engines with coal tenders
•	Lionel 238E & 224E Steam Engines with matching coal cars
•	Lionel Pre-War Locomotive Model 253
•	Lionel 225E Engine & Tender
•	Lionel 2020 Engine with 2020W Pullman (B&O 675)
•	Lionel 6460 Bucyrus Erie Crane Cars (multiple)
•	Lionel 6440 Pullman & 6441 Observation Cars
•	Pair of Lionel 226E Engines with 2666W Coal Cars
•	Pair of Lionel 2343 Engines with 2343C Car
•	Lionel 623, 651, 1666, 2055, 2056, 229, 224 & 2025 Engines
•	Hafner Wind-Up Engines & Cars
•	Williams Delaware & Hudson Engines (802 & 804)
•	Large assortment of Lionel cabooses, log carriers, spotlight cars, animal carriers, crane cars, military sets, and accessories
•	Extensive track lots, switches, transformers, and control equipment
Whether you're a serious Lionel collector or looking to build or expand a layout, this auction offers depth and variety across multiple eras.
 
Sports Card & Memorabilia Highlights:
•	Multiple Upper Deck 1989 Complete Sets
•	Upper Deck 1990 & 1991 Factory Sealed Sets
•	Score 1990 Collector Sets (704-card sets) – several unopened
•	Fleer 1987, 1988, 1989, 1990 Sets (sealed and opened)
•	Topps 1987, 1989, 1990 Baseball Sets
•	Donruss 1988–1992 Sets
•	Football & multi-sport sets including Score 1989 (Sanders & Aikman rookies)
•	Sports posters (40” x 26”), including Notre Dame, vintage baseball & college basketball
•	First Day Stamp Sets & collectible stamp lots

This is a well-rounded collector’s sale with strong representation of post-war Lionel steam power, pre-war pieces, crane cars, passenger cars, and factory card sets from the peak collecting era.

John & Anna Sisk Auction
Thursday March 12th 6PM Timed Auction
12775 E College Ave Coyle OK 73027

Proceed north on Choctaw Road for one mile, then head east on E0780 Road for one block. Enter the property from the south. Look for LRA signs marking the entrance.
5% Buyers Premium will be added to final bid price.
This property comprises approximately 9.04± acres, located just one mile off Highway 105 and seven miles east of Interstate 35, making it easily accessible. The property sits minutes east of Guthrie and I-35, featuring several buildings, including a partially constructed concrete house. It is ideally suited for various commercial uses. This location could be used residentially as well. A small creek and lots of trees provide a bit of privacy. 
Legal Description: 12-16N-01W NW4 NW4 NW4 LESS 1 ACRE FOR SCHOOL LAND
Annual taxes are $379, with a $1,000 exemption applied.
Detailed Property Description
According to the county assessor, the tract consists of approximately nine acres. The access road from Highway 105 to the property is gravel, and the site includes several structures that make the location suitable for commercial development. The seller previously began construction on a concrete house, with specific details on its structure provided upon request. Quonset Round Top approximately 3000 square feet along with a couple of other shops that have electricity.

title: 1994 Spartan II Luxury Show Car
description:
Automotive designer Ron Sparks, designed the Spartan II and made 500 prototypes.  Due to the lack of funding, Mr Sparks sold the rights of the car to luxury auto builder Ned Lamouteaux.

THE SPARTAN II is not a kit car
.  The cars were built in a small factory in San Marcos, Ca. Styled from a 1934 Mercedes 340K and Took 5 Weeks to Build. Spartan's are built on a 1987 Nissian 300ZX platform.

THIS IS A CUSTOM MADE CAR
 There are only 15 coast of paint on the car.6 Gold plated hub caps, and leather interior.  This particular vehicle retailed for 90,000.00 ( they are the thrid owners). 
Because of the large expense, Spartan's were discontinued in 1996.  According to Ron Sprak's son Terry there were only 500 Spartan's built with this being 1 of the last 6 built in 1994

A FEW CELCBRITIES WHO OWNsPARTANS INCLUDED
Joan Collins, Eddie Murphy, Larry Holmes and Tony Doresett. It has benn on shows such as Dallas, Flamingo Road, Matt Houston, and 101 Dalmation's look alike.
A Sheik even bought 2 to go on his American Express Card.  
As of Today , only 15 of these cars are know to exist.

This vehicle was featured on WCIA's "Our Town Rossville" onJuly 1st, 2016
The Vehicle was driven frequently.


This car has been to many car shows, featured on the news many times.  the owner even dressed like Corella De Vil.  The owners have had a blast with this car and now it is time for a new owner.

title: Lakefront Home for Sale at Auction
description:
This well-maintained two-story home on Claytor Lake features a spacious and functional layout with 4 bedrooms, 2 full baths, 2 half baths, and multiple living and entertaining areas. The main level includes a large open kitchen with extensive cabinetry, oversized center island with seating, stainless steel appliances, breakfast nook, formal dining area, living room, sunroom, and 2 bedrooms (one currently suitable as an office). The layout allows for comfortable main level living if desired. It has +/- 3,795 sq ft of living space, with +/- 2,520 on the main level and +/-1,275 on the upper level. Square footage is derived from physical inspection and scanned 3D tour. Home has been used as an AirBnB in the past, with an average rental rate of $396/night.
The second floor offers a large primary bedroom suite with vaulted ceiling, walk-in closets, private bath, an additional oversized bedroom, a full bath, and open-to-below views overlooking the foyer.
Exterior features include a stone and wood finish, paved driveway, whole house generator, detached 2 car garage with stone accents, rear deck, and large patio area ideal for outdoor entertaining. The property also includes access to Claytor Lake, providing convenient enjoyment of boating, fishing, and waterfront recreation. Home sits on +/- 5.56 acres.
The primary dock is a two-story wooden dock that includes an upper sun deck with lounge seating and a lower level designed for boat and kayak storage. A secondary lower deck provides additional space.
The concrete walkway and a series of wooden stairs, all reinforced by heavy-duty stone retaining walls, go to the lake. The entire path is lined with black metal fencing and decorative stone pillars. This layout provides access between the home and the shoreline.
Property is being sold with a 1-year Home Warranty, Standard Plan, which covers heating & air, plumbing, electrical, appliances, and much more.
A shared driveway and associated right-of-way (ROW) provide access to neighboring properties. A portion of this shared access goes through this property. The shared driveway serves as access for the following properties: 3555 Ferncliff Rd, 3545 Ferncliff Rd, 3537 Ferncliff Rd, 3533 Ferncliff Rd, 3521 Ferncliff Rd, and 3515 Ferncliff Rd. Refer to maps in Bidder Pack and Road Maintenance Agreement for location of shared drive.
A drain field easement serving 3533 Ferncliff Road is located at the northwest corner of this property. See map and VA Health Department docs in Bidder Pack.
This property has a road that goes down to the lake and dock areas. A portion of this road goes through neighbor’s property located at 3533 Ferncliff Road. There is a right of way for that road to access dock and lake. 
Property is subject to road maintenance agreement with an annual fee of $200. There may be an additional fee if emergency repairs are needed. refer to road maintenance agreement in bidder pack. Property is also subject to covenants and restrictions. Refer to Covenants and Restrictions in bidder pack. 
Property Features:
- 4 bedrooms
- 2 full baths
- 2 half baths
- +/- 3,795 Sq Ft
- +/- 5.56 acres
- Waterfront on Claytor Lake
- Primary bedroom suite with walk-in closets
- Open kitchen with large island
- Breakfast nook and formal dining area
- Living room and sunroom
- Bedroom/office on main level
- Open foyer with open-to-below upper level
- Rear deck and large patio
- Detached two-car garage
- 2 story dock
Online Auction Ends Friday, March 13th at 3 PM
3537 Ferncliff Rd., Pulaski, VA 24301
PREVIEW DATE: Thursday, February 19th at 4 PM
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com.
DIRECTIONS: From Pulaski, take E Main St towards I-81, turn Left on Old Route 100, take the next Right on to Lowman’s Ferry Rd, turn left onto Ferncliff Rd/Tate Farm Rd just before the bridge, property will be on your left. See signs.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 3 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $10,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before April 27th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the seller up to, but not beyond the Seller's reserve price. Auction starts ending at 3 PM on March 13th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBlueRidge.com

Once your items are ready for shipment, you will receive a notification.When your items are packed, you will receive a final notification with the shipping cost, and you will need to pay for the shipping label. You will also be provided with a tracking number.

Handling Fees: A handling fee of $5 to $10 per package will apply.
USPS Priority boxes: $5 flat fee for handling.
For larger or non-Standard boxes, the handling fee may be up to $10.

Shipping Costs: Shipping costs are based on USPS or UPS rates, plus the handling fee mentioned above.

Please Note: We handle hundreds of packages weekly with a small staff. While we make every effort to ship promptly, please be patient as we work to keep shipping costs low and ensure your items arrive safely. 

Important Notice: You must read and agree to all the terms and conditions before placing any bids. All buyers muct Accept and click "Yes" to confirm your agreement before submitting your bid. Full terms and conditions are available in the Auction Details section under "Terms and Conditions."

**3-Bedroom Ranch Home – Great Investment or Starter Opportunity**

**Seller reserves the right to accept an offer to end auction early before the 1st bid is made or reject a bid. 

Don’t miss this opportunity to purchase a solid 3-bedroom home in a convenient South Bend location. This 1,316 sq ft single-family residence offers a functional layout, established neighborhood setting, and strong potential for rental income or light cosmetic updates.

Property Highlights

* 3 Bedrooms, 1 Bathroom
* 1,316 sq ft living space plus full basement that can be finished to double sq footage
* Built in 1958
* Single-family ranch layout

* Established residential neighborhood
* Ideal for investors, first-time buyers, or downsizers

With solid square footage and a practical floor plan, this property is well-suited for renovation, rental, or owner-occupancy. Conveniently located near shopping, schools, and major roadways.

title: 03-15-2026 Important Fine Art Auction
description:
Important Fine Art Auction

Hibbard • Tobin • Lathrop • Moller • Ferguson

Online Auction  7PM Sunday, March 15, 2026

Bidding opens on or before February 25.

This curated online auction presents an outstanding collection of fine art that spans mediums,
movements, and generations of artistic mastery. The offering includes oil, acrylic, and watercolor
paintings alongside sculptures, expressive sketches, pencil and charcoal works, pastels, and
richly layered mixed media pieces. Both framed and unframed works are available, allowing
collectors flexibility in presentation and investment. Styles range from Impressionistic and
abstract compositions to realism, still life, landscape, and beyond, creating a dynamic and
visually compelling catalog.
Highlights include works by William Hugh Ferguson, celebrated for his refined academic
technique and sensitive portraiture, with a special emphasis on his beautifully executed
sketches that reveal his mastery of form, light, and human expression. These works on paper
offer a rare glimpse into Ferguson’s creative process, capturing movement and emotion with
remarkable economy and precision. The auction also features Western watercolor paintings by
Allen M. Dean, known for his atmospheric depictions of frontier life and expansive landscapes
that embody the spirit of the American West.
Sculptural works by internationally recognized artist Steve Tobin bring bold organic forms and
powerful presence, while pieces by Aldro T. Hibbard reflect the rich tradition of American
Impressionism with luminous color and dynamic brushwork. Works by Zvi Mairovich offer
striking contemporary abstraction with energetic texture and movement, complemented by the
refined modernist sensibilities of Klaus Ihlenfeld. The collection further includes compelling
works by Stephen George Maniatty, Hans Moller, and noted American painter William Langson
Lathrop, whose landscapes helped shape early 20th-century Impressionism in the United
States.
Rounding out the auction is a finely detailed KPM hand-painted portrait plaque, representing the
renowned German porcelain tradition prized for its craftsmanship and artistic excellence.
Together, this diverse and thoughtfully assembled collection offers something for every collector,
from museum-quality names to expressive works full of character and history. Whether you are
seeking a timeless classic, a striking contemporary piece, or rare artist studies, this auction
provides an exceptional opportunity to acquire beautiful and meaningful art.

Aldro Thompson Hibbard (American, 1886–1972)
Prominent Rockport School painter celebrated for bold impasto New England winter landscapes and one of the leading Massachusetts Impressionists.

Steve Tobin (American, b. 1957)
Internationally recognized contemporary sculptor known for monumental bronze, glass, and steel works exploring nature and growth forms, including his well-known Root series exhibited in museums and public spaces worldwide.

William Langson Lathrop (American, 1859–1938)
Important founding member of the New Hope Art Colony; American Impressionist known for atmospheric Pennsylvania river landscapes and seasonal rural views.

Hans Moller (American, 1857–1925)
German-born American painter active in Philadelphia and New Hope circles, recognized for Tonalist landscapes and coastal scenes with moody, subdued palettes.

William H. Ferguson (American, 1850–1923) (over 100 pieces from this artist)
Pennsylvania Impressionist-associated landscape painter active in Philadelphia, noted for atmospheric rural and river scenes influenced by late-19th-century American tonalism.

Klaus Ihlenfeld (German-American, 1934–2005)
Sculptor and designer known for cast-bronze furniture and sculptural forms built from dense, organic clusters resembling reeds and natural growth. Active in the United States in the late 20th century, his work blends modernist design with fine art and is collected as both decorative art and sculpture.

Plus many additional listed and unlisted artists – original oils, lithographs, and framed works – offering collecting opportunities in every price range.

Preview by appointment, call 610-799-0808

Items are located at Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Rt. 309, Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Pick up hours at the gallery following the auction:  12noon-3:30pm the day after the auction and Monday thru Friday from 9:30AM-3:30PM  daily.

All items MUST be removed within 7 DAYS of the auction.

Local deliver available. Please call us at 610-799-0808 for information.

Online Only Bidding: Auction starts closing with 2 items closing per minute, bidding continues as auction closes.Extended bidding, if a bid is placed within the last 1 minute of a lot’s scheduled close time the bidding will be extended 1 minute from the last bid.

Buying at auction is not like buying new; the items are used, vintage and antique. They are consigned by estates and people downsizing. They have been used in people’s homes for their purposes, so while most items are in good condition, most are not in perfect condition. We provide private previews for the public to come to the gallery and physically inspect items being sold. If you cannot be here for the inspection, please contact us by email for additional photos or more detailed condition reports.

We try to present items to bidders fairly; however, it is the bidder’s duty to make independent investigation of the condition of items before bidding.

Online bidding Terms:  Major Credit Cards, 20% Buyer’s Premium, 6% Sales Tax collected if you have provided a valid Sales Tax Exemption Certificate. 

IMPORTANT PLEASE READ PAYMENT TERMS FOR THIS AUCTION 

All major credit cards are accepted. Your credit  card will immediatley be charged for all purchases. 

However at auctioneers discretion INVOICES EXCEEDING $1200 may required payment by wire transfer or cash. 

Do You Have Something to Sell? We Can Help!

Real Estate Sales, Downsizing & Estate Services

Business Liquidations, Jewelry, Couture, Motorcycles & Car Collections

Estate Appraisals

title: Westside Moving Auction - Springfield IL
description:
Westside Moving Auction - Springfield IL
Featuring: 450+ Lots featuring: Quality Furniture, Décor, Christmas, & More
Address: 3500 Sandwood Dr, Springfield IL 62707

Auction Ends: Sunday March 15th at 7:00 PM CST (Soft Close)

In-Person Preview: Thursday March 12th 4pm - 6pm
3500 Sandwood Dr, Springfield IL 62707

Item Pick-Up: Tuesday March 17th, 1:00pm - 6:00pm
Pick-up location: 3500 Sandwood Dr, Springfield IL 62707

Pickup appointments must be scheduled via Shipping Saint 
(email/text link provided after auction ends).

Ben Ladage Auctions - Auburn, IL
Office: 217-652-0924
View full catalog and bid at: www.benladage.com
Please read the complete Terms & Conditions before placing any bids.
________________________________________
BUYER'S PREMIUM & PAYMENT:

A 15% buyer's premium will be added to each item.
We offer a 3% discount for cash, check, or non-credit card payment - you must notify us before the auction closes to receive this discount.

Accepted payment forms: Visa, Mastercard, Discover, debit card, cash, or approved checks.
No checks accepted without prior approval or established business history. Checks with bank letter of credit may be accepted-please call our office in advance.

Invoices for items will be billed to your registered credit card immediately following the auction. If shipping is selected, you will receive a separate invoice once shipping costs are calculated.
________________________________________
FOLLOWING THE AUCTION:

Winning bidders will receive both an email and text message with the option to select pickup or shipping.
o If pickup is selected, you'll receive full pickup instructions, including date, time, and location based on the information you provided during registration.
o If shipping is selected, your items will be securely packed by our in-house shipping department. Once packaging is complete, you will receive another email and text message with the total shipping cost and handling fee.
________________________________________
SHIPPING:

Shipping is available on most items and provided in-house by Ben Ladage Auctions using Shipping Saint. After the auction:
"	You will receive an email and text to choose either shipping or pick-up.
"	If shipping is selected, items will be packaged, and you will receive a second notification with shipping and handling costs.
"	We use USPS, UPS, and FedEx and always select the most cost-effective method.
"	Please allow up to 15 business days for items to ship after the pickup date, although most packages go out within 5 business days.
We do not ship large or heavy items unless pre-approved prior to auction closing. We do not accept outside shipping labels or provide shipping quotes. Shipping costs are the responsibility of the buyer and are non-refundable.
________________________________________
ITEM DESCRIPTIONS, INSPECTIONS, & DISCLAIMERS:

All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no warranties, guarantees, refunds, or returns of any kind. Descriptions including condition, age, origin, or rarity are offered as opinion only. The bidder assumes all responsibility for determining value and authenticity.
Preview and inspection are strongly encouraged. The auctioneer is not responsible for errors in descriptions or missed bids (online, absentee, phone, or otherwise). All announcements on auction day take precedence over any prior marketing.
________________________________________
PICKUP POLICY - READ CAREFULLY:

All items must be picked up on the scheduled date and time. If you cannot make the scheduled pickup, please do not bid.
Items not picked up are forfeited and become property of Ben Ladage Auctions unless other arrangements have been made in advance.
We understand life happens and will try to work with you-but communication is key. If we have to retrieve your items from the auction site and transport them back to Auburn, a minimum fee of $25 per item will be charged. This fee helps cover time, labor, and tracking logistics.
________________________________________
ABSENTEE BIDDING & TECHNICAL INFORMATION:

Don't want to watch the auction live? The best strategy is to leave your maximum bid ahead of time. Our system will bid incrementally on your behalf.
This is a soft close auction. If a bid is placed in the last 3 minutes of a lot's closing, that lot will automatically extend by 3 minutes. All lots close in staggered intervals.
If you're unsure how to bid online, contact our office before the final day-we're happy to help! Absentee bids are accepted and will include the 15% buyer's premium.
________________________________________

Sellers: Shirley Antonacci / Jenny Rich 

We appreciate your bids and support-thank you for participating in this online auction!

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Your Team at The UPS Store
5257 Buckeystown Pike
Frederick, MD 21704
Phone: 301-620-8825 Fax: 301-620-8821
Website: https://www.theupsstore.com/5681
Email: store5681@theupsstore.com

Subscribe to our Newsletter and follow us on Facebook to become a UsedTwo Auction Insider, gaining access to pre-auction Sneak Peeks and more.

Please ensure UsedTwo communications are not directed to your spam folder. We aim to promptly notify you of winning bids.

For any questions or concerns, please email team@usedtwo.com.

title: Tommy Shomo Estate on Hampden Sydney Campus
description:
We are proud to offer the sale of items from the estate of Hampden Sydney's beloved assistant Dean of Students and a man who so much more to the campus, the late Tommy Shomo (Class of '69). 

The estate is full of unique items from Mr. Shomo's collection including a vast library of interesting books (many stamped with his library seal), nicely framed art, Asian antiques, bone china, American antiques, Biggs and Virginia Craftsmen furniture, Chippendale, Federal, Hipplewhite, and Queen Anne style furniture, outdoor furniture and items from his beautiful and cherished garden, a 2010 Ford Ranger pickup truck, sterling silver items, housewares, Persian rugs, tools, and so much more. 

More than that, there are items to remember Mr. Shomo by like teacups, champagne flutes, and other items that you might have seen at his famous etiquette dinners. As well as tools you may have seem him using while keeping his perfect little side-yard garden. 

Bidding on this estate will open when the photgraphy is complete and close beginning at 6:00PM Saturday, March 21st. All items must be picked up Sunday, March 22nd between 12:00 and 7:00 PM.

title: Two Prominant Estates
description:
Thank you for registering for our auction!  

Two prominant Estate Auctions
Caroline Ireland Estate in Lynn Haven, FL. and a $1.7 million dollar home (sold) in Panama City Beach, FL.

Ireland Estate Auction   Lynn Haven, Florida   2/22/2026-3/22/2026
This auction features a carefully curated collection of Antiques, Sculpture, Art, Gold, Sterling Silver, one of a kind Artisan Pottery pieces and much more from the Estate of Caroline Ireland.
Mrs. Ireland was known for her exquisite taste in fine art, including sculpture, artisan pottery, functional art, rugs and antiques. More importantly she was nationally known for her philanthropic activaties, President of the Caroline P. and Charles W Ireland Foundation, Her community service includes leadership positions that often focus on Education and the Arts serving on: The Board of Trustees for Whittier College, 1996 to 2012. The Lynn Haven Public Library Advisory Board, 1984-1987. The Board of the Panama City Episcopal Day School. Lynn Haven Public Building Board Chairman, 1970. Northwest Regional Library Board (Northwest Florida), 1963-1970. Founder of the Friends of the Bay County Public Libraries, Inc. Fund Raising Board Chairman for Lynn Haven Public Library Expansion. Building, 1981-1988. University of Alabama President's Council, 1970's. Member of the President's Council, University of Florida, 1989-1993 Advisory Board, New Orleans Museum of Art, 1991-1993. Founding member of the Sam P. Harn Museum, University of Florida, 1989. Member of the Advisory Board, Birmingham Museum of Art, Birmingham, Alabama, 1987 Chair of the Birmingham Museum of Art 30th Anniversary Ball, 1986. Member of the American Council for the Arts. Other service organizations she served and supported include: The Extension Board of the Chicago Maternity Center, 1960-1986 Board member of Protestant Baby Home, New Orleans, 1953 1954 Junior League of America chapters in New Orleans, Chicago and Birmingham. In 1984 Caroline and her late husband, Charles established the Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland Endowment for Scholarly Distinction at the University of Alabama, Birmingham. The endowment supports two programs, The Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland Award for Scholarly Distinction that recognizes the scholarly achievement in the arts and sciences by a current UAB faculty member and the Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland. Distinguished Visiting Scholar Award that brings outstanding scholars who are generally recognized as figures of distinction in the arts and sciences to the UAB campus. In addition, the Caroline P. and Charles W. Ireland International Students Fund supports the university's international students. Most recently, she established the Caroline P. Ireland Endowed Award for Exceptional Students and Projects in Graduate Studies. This award fosters a commitment to research and discovery by providing up to ten scholarships annually to master degree students. Mrs. Ireland's support of UAB includes funding a conference room in the Reynolds Historical Library named in her honor, a major portion of the ninth floor in the West Pavilion, known as the Camellia Pavilion, as well as contributing to the Alys Robinson Stephens Performing Arts Center, the Department of Art and Art History, the School of Nursing and the Camellia Medical Group.

Panama City Beach Estate
Million dollar Prominant estate with high quality furnishings, housewares, art, tools, and more!  Located in River Camps near airport in Panama City Beach.  

18% BP

title: Recreational Retreat for Sale in Giles County
description:
Recreational Retreat for Sale in Giles County
Check out this property that is surrounded by the Jefferson National Forest. Property offering privacy, scenic views, and abundant wildlife. Owned by the same family since 1961, it features miles of recently cleared and maintained four-wheeler trails, 10 ponds (several spring-fed), multiple potential cabin sites, and the option to live fully off the grid.
Sitting at nearly 3,000 feet in elevation, the property provides views and a true connection to nature. Despite its elevation, the land lives up to the name Flat Top Mountain, with an impressive amount of level to gently rolling land. Improvements include several existing buildings, a generator shed, and an original log cabin that is over 100 years old.
Recreational opportunities are endless, and the property is conveniently located near Dismal Falls and extensive Jefferson National Forest trail systems. Don’t miss out!
Online Auction Ending Wednesday, March 25th, at 4 PM
TBD Flat Top Rd., Pearisburg, VA 24134

title: 1,280 Acres Near Oakwood, OK - Fred Holsapple Estate
description:
Presenting the Fred Holsapple Estate - a rare opportunity to own a truly versatile piece of Oklahoma countryside, offered at auction. Whether you're a beginning producer ready to put down roots, an established operator looking to expand, an investor seeking strong potential, or a buyer dreaming of recreational land or the perfect homesite, this property checks every box.

While being cross-fenced, the land offers flexibility for grazing on native grass acres or the option to incorporate cropland grazing with ease. Abundant water resources - including solar wells, electric wells, and multiple runoff ponds - provide reliability and support for livestock and land management.

Adding to its appeal, wind turbines located on Tracts 1 and 6 create an additional income stream, blending agricultural use with long-term investment potential. With most tracts having been idle for the past few years, buyers will appreciate the freedom and clean slate this property offers for future plans, improvements, and customized management.

Don't miss your chance to own part of this exceptional estate. The auction will be held Thursday, March 26, at 10:00 a.m. at the Oakwood Skating Rink.

title: Country Home & Acreage for Sale off The Blue Ridge Parkway
description:
Check out this beautifully designed 3 bed, 2.5 bath home offering +/- 3,182 sq ft of finished living space with a layout that provides both comfort and flexibility. Home is situated on +/- 88 acres of mostly open land with a barn, spring fed pond, views, and seclusion.
The main level provides an abundant space for both everyday living and entertainment. It has +/- 2,372 sq ft and it includes a spacious great room, separate living room, formal dining room, a half-bath, kitchen, and an Eat In Kitchen—offering multiple gathering areas to suit any occasion. You can also find the master bedroom and a full bath on this level. There is also a fireplace in the great room. Enjoy the large composite deck—perfect for relaxing, grilling, or entertaining guests.
The basement level includes 2 bedrooms, 1 full bath, 2 storage areas, and direct access to the attached two-bay garage. This level offers approximately +/- 810 sq ft of finished space and +/- 2,292 sq ft of unfinished space, providing storage or future expansion potential. You will also find a wood stove down in the basement for another source of heat.
With the brick exterior and classic shingle roof create timeless curb appeal. Throughout the home, you’ll find a blend of carpet, hardwood, and tile flooring. Served by well and septic systems, along with a heat pump and a concrete foundation.
The beautiful brick home sits on +/- 88.2 acres, the property features a barn, beautiful open fields (currently used as corn fields), several outbuildings, and stunning views of the Blue Ridge Mountains. A portion of the property is fenced and also features a spring fed pond! This property is right off the Blue Ridge Parkway, making it great access to nature. The property’s access is between Milepost 163 and Milepost 164.
Property is accessed by private drive Soapstone Hill Road off the Blue Ridge Parkway. According to Floyd County GIS Map, VDOT (Virginia Department of Transportation) map, and survey this road continues between the two tracts offered to Roberson Mill Road and Hope Road. The road is overgrown and unmaintained. According to VDOT, the road has been discontinued. There is also a 50' Right of Way that goes along edge of adjacent tract located at 2332 Woods Gap Road. This is a Right of Way only. No road has been built. If New Owner wants access from Woods Gap Road, they would need to build road within Right of Way. Refer to maps in Bidder Pack.
Property is being sold with a 1-year Home Warranty, Standard Plan, which covers heating & air, plumbing, electrical, appliances, and much more. Click on link below for more details.

title: 3 Bed 1.5 Bath Home/Mini Farm on 6+/- Acres
description:
Due to relocation issues we will be postponing theis auction until Mar 28th 2026.
 
Jackie Mills is relocating and offering her beautiful 6± acre mini farm on the edge of New Douglas, IL, and personal property at auction! This charming property features a  3-bedroom, 1.5-bath home with appliances, plus a tractor, side-by-side, greenhouse, and multiple outbuildings. The land is perfect for small-scale farming or hobby living, with pasture, garden areas, and space for animals. Jackie raised chickens, produce, and flowers here—now it’s your turn to enjoy peaceful country living and begin your  journey. Open House Nov 30th 12-2 PM

title: On-Site Forest City
description:
On-Site Forest City — The Collection of Donald Butler

We are honored to present The Collection of Donald Butler, an exceptional on-site estate auction in Forest City, North Carolina. This sale features a thoughtfully curated selection of beloved personal possessions from the estate of Donald Baxter “Don” Butler (1938–2025), whose lifelong appreciation for quality, craftsmanship, and history is reflected throughout this distinguished collection.Please read all information prior to bidding. 

Payment
New customers with invoices exceeding $1,000 must pay by cash or Zelle (ACH) $2.00 surcharge on all Zelle payments. Established clients may pay by cash, approved check, credit card, or debit card.Credit and debit cards on file will be automatically charged within three (3) hours of the auction ending.Invoices over $1,000 must be paid through LiveAuctioneers invoicing, Zelle, or cash. We do not accept credit card payments over the phone due to chargeback fraud.

Important
We strongly recommend previewing all items prior to purchase.All mechanical items are sold as-is, where-is, with no warranties or guarantees. Please request additional photos or information before bidding if needed.

Pick-Up Information
Pick-up dates are March 28 through April 1, from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM.Items not picked up by April 1 at 4:00 PM will be transported to our facility in Weaverville, NC.If we must transport your item, a surcharge will apply:$30 per standard item$50 per large item (scooters, motorcycles, single-lot bicycles, etc.)If you need a vehicle, tractor, or other large item picked up after April 1, arrangements must be made prior to the auction. We may not be able to accommodate late requests.

Shipping
If you require shipping for a small item or group of small items, a $25 per item surcharge will apply to transport the item(s) from the auction site to our facility and deliver them to the shipper.Not all items are eligible for shipping.Shipping costs charged by the shipping company are separate and the responsibility of the buyer.Group LotsAny large group lots not picked up by Monday, April 1 will be considered abandoned.Abandoned items will be surrendered and no refunds will be issued.

Auction address and pick up location'
471 Morrow Motor Rd
Forest City NC 28043

Infor@brokenarchgallery.com
828-713-8869
NCFL #9399
NCAL #9739

title: Brian Gordon Vehicle and Toy Tractor Collection
description:
Following the loss of his eyesight five years ago, Brian has chosen to share his extraordinary collection of vehicles, tractor and toy tractors—comprising more than 300 unique pieces—by offering them at auction. Each toy tractor has been thoughtfully selected and lovingly cared for, reflecting Brian’s enduring passion for agricultural history and craftsmanship. This rare opportunity is perfect for collectors, enthusiasts, and families alike.

15% Buyers premium added to final bid price

title: UsedTwo Auction 82
description:
Welcome to UsedTwo Auctions. Preview images of catalog items will become available as upcoming auctions are processed. The complete Auction Catalog, including all preview images, will be available by Monday evening prior to the commencement of the auction.

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Your Team at The UPS Store
5257 Buckeystown Pike
Frederick, MD 21704

Website: https://www.theupsstore.com/5681

Subscribe to our Newsletter and follow us on Facebook to become a UsedTwo Auction Insider, gaining access to pre-auction Sneak Peeks and more.

Please ensure UsedTwo communications are not directed to your spam folder. We aim to promptly notify you of winning bids.

For any questions or concerns, please email team@usedtwo.com.

title: Real Estate Auction - Tazewell County, Virginia
description:
Due to the inclement weather, which prevented prospective buyers from looking at the property, the auction end date has been postponed. The auction now ends on April 1st at 4 PM.

Property Overview:

+/-122.36 acres of open farmland in Tazewell County VA. Located on Barnes Lane off Route # 61 / East Riverside Drive.
The property has three barns, a brick-and-stone silo, multiple sheds, and outbuildings suitable for equipment and livestock. Fully fenced. Former dairy operation and currently used for cattle grazing.
The land has moderate topography with open fields, pasture, good building sites, and long-range views of the Blue Ridge Mountains. Suitable for continued agricultural use or private farm ownership.
Approximately 11 acres on the western portion of the property are within the Town of Tazewell limits. The interactive map link below shows town limits, aerial imagery, and property boundaries.

Property Features:

-±122.36 acres of open farmland
-Three barns and brick-and-stone silo
-Multiple sheds and outbuildings
-Fully fenced
-Former dairy farm: currently cattle pasture
-Moderate topography
-Good building sites
-Blue Ridge Mountain views
-Portion located within Town of Tazewell

Important Property Information:

Road Maintenance Agreement: Property access is via a 50-foot right-of-way from Route # 61 (East Riverside Drive) and is subject to a Road Maintenance Agreement. Purchaser acknowledges review and agrees the agreement will be executed and recorded at closing.
Right-of-Way: A 15-foot shared right-of-way runs along the western boundary near the barns and provides access to the upper portion of the property. Shared with the neighboring property at 319 Barnes Lane.
Water Source: Concrete water trough is supplied by a spring located on adjoining property. No water or spring rights will convey.
Land Use Assessment: Property is currently taxed under land use assessment. Purchaser is responsible for applying for continuation and for any rollback taxes if disqualified or not filed. 122.36 acres being sold is currently in land use taxation. When a property is in land use, real estate taxes are discounted. According to Tazewell County, the current annual real estate tax amount under land use is $337.56. The purchase contract states that the new owner will either keep property in land use, or pay the rollback taxes if taken out of land use. The estimated rollback tax if taken out of land use is $7,800 according to Tazewell County.
Online Auction Ends Wednesday, April 1st at 4 PM
230 Barnes Ln., North Tazewell, VA 24630
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Realtor Bill Cordle (276) 701-3446 or email billcordle1@gmail.com
DIRECTIONS: From Tazewell, take Route 16 S to Route # 61 (East Riverside Dr). Turn left on Route # 61, then right on Barnes Ln. Property ahead. 230 Barnes Lane, North Tazewell 24630
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property
information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $10,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before May 18th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on April 1st, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBlueRidge.com

title: UsedTwo Auction 83
description:
Welcome to UsedTwo Auctions. Preview images of catalog items will become available as upcoming auctions are processed. The complete Auction Catalog, including all preview images, will be available by Monday evening prior to the commencement of the auction.

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Your Team at The UPS Store
5257 Buckeystown Pike
Frederick, MD 21704
Phone: 301-620-8825 Fax: 301-620-8821
Website: https://www.theupsstore.com/5681
Email: store5681@theupsstore.com

Subscribe to our Newsletter and follow us on Facebook to become a UsedTwo Auction Insider, gaining access to pre-auction Sneak Peeks and more.

Please ensure UsedTwo communications are not directed to your spam folder. We aim to promptly notify you of winning bids.

For any questions or concerns, please email team@usedtwo.com.

title: Mobile Home Park and Land for Sale in Bassett, VA
description:
Searching for your next real estate investment opportunity? Check out this mobile home park for sale in Bassett, VA. This property features +/- 8.58 acres and is serviced by public water and a private septic system. Property is minutes away from Philpott Dam making it great for boating, fishing, and hiking. 
There are currently 9 tenants, all operating under month-to-month verbal rental agreements. This arrangement provides flexibility for a new owner, who may determine which tenants remain and which may be asked to vacate. Rental rates may also be adjusted at the buyer’s discretion.
Currently, 9 renters occupy a total of 10 out of the 26 available lots. One tenant rents 2 lots to allow for additional space. All tenants are current on their rent. Each tenant pays $250 per month per lot, except for the tenant leasing 2 lots, who pays a total of $400 per month. Total monthly income is $2,400 per month. Owner recently spent over $40,000 to have the park cleaned up and abandoned mobile homes removed. 
All mobile homes located on the property are owned by the tenants, not the park owner. Therefore, mobile home titles will not be transferred to purchaser. Additionally, purchaser acknowledges that there may be limitations or regulations that prohibit the relocation / removal of mobile homes from property. Bidder acknowledges that information on manufactured homes is limited to physical exterior inspection and that the seller does not possess titles to the mobile homes. Inspection of mobile homes is only relevant to ascertain overall appeal of mobile home park. 
Auction is subject to VA Statute 55.1-1308.2. Seller must notify all tenants in writing of the intent to sell at least 60 days prior to selling. Tenants will have those 60 days to purchase property at an agreeable price to seller. More information on the code can be found at https:/law.lis.virginia.gov/vacode/title55.1/chapterl3/section55.1-1308.2/ 
Online Auction Ends Tuesday, April 21st at 4PM
15 Spruce Pine Ln., Bassett, VA 24055
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Realtor Darin Greear with Long and Foster Blacksburg VA Realty at 540-320-5859 or email Darin@RinerVA.com
DIRECTIONS: From Bassett, take VA-57 for approximately 3.5 miles. Property is on the left, see sign. 
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $5,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before June 22nd, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on April 21st, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. title: Acreage for Sale on Little Reed Island Creek
description:
Acreage for Sale on Little Reed Island Creek
Discover this +/-177.5 acre wooded property with extensive frontage along Little Reed Island Creek. Featuring approximately 1.7 miles of creek frontage. 1,800+ feet of the creek flows through the property, land is on both sides. This land offers a rare and scenic natural setting.
The woods provide opportunities for recreation, hunting, wildlife enjoyment, and could be a great place to build your home. Ideal as a private hunting retreat, recreational getaway, or long-term investment, the property offers outstanding seclusion. Down near the creek, there is a gazebo great for relaxing! Electricity is available to the property.
Little Reed Island Creek adds to the property’s appeal with quality fishing for rainbow and brown trout. Locally treated as a trout stream, it supports both stocked and wild trout. Seasonal kayaking and canoeing are also possible, depending on water levels.
There is a road / trail that goes through property to adjacent properties, but title search indicates there are no legal right of ways granted through this property to those properties. It will be up to new owner to allow or prohibit travel along that road.
Property being sold was once owned by PC Phosphate Company before being sold in 1997. There has not been any mining activities since, and owner is not aware of the extent or activity of any mining on the property prior to 1997. Mineral rights have not been severed and convey with the property.
Property Features:
·        ±177.5 acres of wooded land
·        1.7 miles of frontage along Little Reed Island Creek
·        Additional 1,800+ feet of Little Reed Island Creek running through the property
·        Scenic creekfront and interior water features
·        Excellent hunting opportunities
·        Natural habitat for deer, turkey, and other wildlife
·        Ideal for recreation and outdoor activities
·        Private and secluded setting

title: UsedTwo Auction 84
description:
Welcome to UsedTwo Auctions. Preview images of catalog items will become available as upcoming auctions are processed. The complete Auction Catalog, including all preview images, will be available by Monday evening prior to the commencement of the auction.

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Frederick, MD 21704

title: 5/5 MEIER BEEF & GRAIN, LLP AUCTION
description:
160± ACRES * BLAINE COUNTY, OKLAHOMA * OKEENE AREA * GRASS PASTURE
CROP LAND * GOOD CATTLE FARM * POND * CREEK * HIGHWAY FRONTAGE
MEIER BEEF & GRAIN, LLP AUCTION
TUESDAY MAY 5TH		10:00AM
AUCTION LOCATION: Onsite, HWY 3 & E0710 Rd Hitchcock, OK, 73744
This very nice quarter section of land consists of approximately 160± acres more or less according to the county assessor. According to the county FSA office there is 155.60 farmland with 90.13 cropland, 90.13 wheat base and 30 PLC yield. This property does have rural water, nice size pond and creek. There was new fence installed in 2022 with steel corners/gates. Principal soils are Port Clay Loam, 0-1% slopes, class II; Kingfisher Silt Loam, 1-3% slopes, class III; Yahola Sandy Loam, 0-1% slopes, class V and McClain Silty Loam,0-1% slopes, class I; Kingfisher-Lucien Complex, 3-8% slopes, class IV
Directions to the Farm: From the intersection of Hwy 51 & 8 in Okeene, Ok. Go south 8.5 miles, watch for signs, corner of Hwy 8 & CR 710 
Legal Description: SE/4 of 36-18N-11 W.I.M. Blaine County, OK.
Taxes: $449.00
Possession: will be after the harvest of the existing crop
Minerals: Selling surface rights only
Pre-Auction Offers: No pre-auction offers will be accepted prior to auction date.
Pre-viewing the Farm: This can be done on your own during daylight house only.
Internet Bidding: will be available by logging on to www.lippardauctions.com to register. Neither the auction company nor the seller is responsible in the event of loss of internet signal by either side.
Terms: 10% of the total purchase price is to be placed in escrow the day of the auction with the balance being due upon delivery of good title. 
Disclaimer: All information has been taken from sources believed reliable; however, no guarantee is being made by the auction company nor the seller. Bidders should satisfy themselves as to any inspections prior to bidding. Financing, if being used must be in place prior to bidding. Any announcements made the day of the auction shall supersede all marketing or previous comments.

SELLER: MEIER BEEF & GRAIN, LLP

title: Barn Finds Auto Auction
description: Step back in time with the Barn Finds Auto Auction, a rare opportunity to bid on a collection of vintage, collectible vehicles that have been tucked away in barns and outbuildings for years, patiently waiting for their next chapter. This auction showcases true automotive time capsules, including standout classics like a 1970 Dodge Dude and a 1958 Nash, along with a variety of other collectible vehicles and automobilia. Whether you’re a seasoned collector, restorer, or enthusiast looking for an authentic barn find, this auction is packed with character, history, and potential, offering pieces you don’t come across every day.

description: Go 2 Guys Auction Marketplace is your premier platform to sell high-value items with ease. Designed for consignors, our marketplace allows you to list items with a set price, providing a straightforward and transparent selling experience. We accept items with a minimum value of $5,000, with a $500 fee to list and park your item at our lot, this fee is waived once your item sells. title: Taxiway Drive Auction
description:
Online Only Absolute Auction

Bidding Ends June 18th 2:00pm CST

263 W Taxiway Dr, Albany, KY 42602

Dale Hollow Lakefront: Home + 4 Lots + Guns & Ammo

TJ Stearns, Agent, 606-396-1177
Hunter Shearer, Broker/Auctioneer, 606-306-2995

Lakeview Home and four vacant lots on Dale Hollow Lake. This turnkey offering features a single-family home with stunning lake views and four adjacent vacant lots included in the auction, providing outstanding development and investment potential in a coveted lake community. Enjoy panoramic Dale Hollow Lake vistas from multiple vantage points, with outdoor living spaces ideal for entertaining and relaxing as the sun sets over the water. The property sits just 9 minutes from Wolf River Dock for boating and fishing, 10 minutes to downtown Albany, KY for shops and dining, and 16 minutes to Wisdom Dock Resort for marina amenities and recreational activities. 

Tract #1: 0.971+/- acre lakeview parcel that offers an extraordinary opportunity to build your custom sanctuary with breathtaking water views that will never get old! This pristine vacant lot provides nearly a full acre of prime real estate with coveted lakeview positioning, unlimited design potential, and a place to create your own personal oasis. Whether you're dreaming of a modern lakehouse masterpiece, or cozy cabin retreat,  this exceptional property lets you wake up to stunning sunrise reflections and evening sunsets over the water while experiencing the peace that only lakefront living can provide.

Tract #2: Charming 3-bedroom, 2 1/2 -bath home on 0.370+/- acres, perfectly positioned just off the airport runway. Embrace breathtaking views of the stunning Dale Hollow Lake from your own backyard, ensuring you can soak in nature's beauty every day.

Inside, the home boasts an array of custom touches designed for both comfort and low maintenance. The intelligent layout ensures both functionality and style, making these residences ideal for year-round living or an idyllic getaway. 

Originally crafted as a hangar home, the design offers a unique opportunity to convert it back to its original purpose, perfect for aviation enthusiasts or those seeking a distinct living experience. Don’t miss your chance to own a slice of paradise where lake living and adventure meet!

Tract #3: 0.748 acres+/- acres of prime vacant land. A blank canvas, ready for you to create your own oasis.

Tract #4: Discover 0.636+/- acres of raw space with endless development possibilities. This vacant lot is ideal for a custom home, backyard retreat, or small multi-use project.

Tract #5: This vacant lot offers 0.642+/-  acres of buildable space. Perfect for a single-family custom home with a yard, or a compact development project.
